semantic_summary: Shows how to embed a media file (SWF) as a ScreenAnnotation in a PDF and later retrieve the embedded media resource via the annotation's RenditionAction and FileSpecification.
rules: To embed a media file as a screen annotation: create a {ScreenAnnotation} with a target {rect} and the media file path, then add it to {page}.Annotations.

To extract the embedded media from a screen annotation: cast the annotation to {ScreenAnnotation}, obtain its Action as {RenditionAction}, retrieve the {Rendition}, cast to {MediaRendition}, get the {MediaClip}, cast to {MediaClipData}, and read the media bytes from {FileSpecification}.Contents.
When accessing the contents of a {FileSpecification}, read the stream in chunks (e.g., 1024‑byte buffer) to obtain the raw byte array of the embedded resource.
warnings: The example uses Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle; ensure the correct namespace is imported.
The annotation is accessed by index (Annotations[1]); in real code, locate the annotation by type or other identifier.

Access the first page safely: if ({doc}.Pages.Count > 0) {page} = {doc}.Pages[1]; // 1‑based indexing
Create a rectangle for annotation placement: {rect} = new Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle({int}, {int}, {int}, {int});

Add a TextAnnotation: {annotation} = new Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.TextAnnotation({page}, {rect}) { Title = {string_literal}, Contents = {string_literal}, Color = Aspose.Pdf.Color.{color} }; {annotation}.Border = new Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.Border({annotation}) { Width = {int} }; {page}.Annotations.Add({annotation});

Iterate over all annotations on a page: for (int i = 1; i <= {page}.Annotations.Count; i++) {{ var ann = {page}.Annotations[i]; var title = ann is Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.MarkupAnnotation ma ? ma.Title : "N/A"; /* use ann.AnnotationType, ann.Contents, title */ }}
Delete an annotation by its 1‑based index: {page}.Annotations.Delete({int});
Save the modified document to PDF: {doc}.Save({output_pdf});

Demonstrates how to create a FreeText annotation with a callout line (FreeTextCallout intent), configure its appearance, rich text content, and ending style, and also how to import such an annotation from an XFDF file.
rules: To add a callout free‑text annotation: create {doc}, add a {page}, define a {rect} and a {da} (DefaultAppearance) with {color} and {float} font size, instantiate FreeTextAnnotation(page, rect, da), set Intent = FreeTextIntent.FreeTextCallout, set EndingStyle = LineEnding.OpenArrow (or other), assign Callout = new Point[] { {point_array} }, optionally set RichText = {string_literal}, add the annotation to page.Annotations, then save {doc} to {output_pdf}.

To import a callout free‑text annotation from XFDF: build an XFDF string containing a <freetext> element with attributes intent="FreeTextCallout", callout-line="{point_array}" (comma‑separated coordinates), tail="{string_literal}" for the ending style, and optional <contents-richtext> and <defaultappearance> nodes, then call Document.ImportAnnotationsFromXfdf(stream) on {input_pdf} and save to {output_pdf}.
warnings: Rectangle and Point types belong to Aspose.Pdf, not System.Drawing.
The example requires Aspose.PDF for .NET version 19.6 or later.
When creating XFDF manually, the attribute name for the callout line must be "callout-line" (not just "callout").

rules: Load a PDF document from {input_pdf} using new Document(string path).
Delete all bookmarks by invoking {doc}.Outlines.Delete().
Save the modified document to {output_pdf} using {doc}.Save(string path).

semantic_summary: Shows how to remove all bookmarks from an existing PDF document using the PdfBookmarkEditor class and save the result to a new file.
rules: Load a PDF document with PdfBookmarkEditor.BindPdf({input_pdf}).
Delete all bookmarks in the loaded document using PdfBookmarkEditor.DeleteBookmarks().
Write the modified PDF to a file with PdfBookmarkEditor.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: DeleteBookmarks removes every bookmark; there is no overload for selective deletion.
The output path must be writable and the input PDF must exist.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to open a PDF document, delete a specific bookmark (outline) by its title, and save the modified document.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} using new Document({input_pdf}).
Delete a bookmark from {doc}.Outlines by calling Delete({string_literal}) where the literal matches the bookmark title.
Persist changes by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to programmatically expand all bookmarks (outlines) in a PDF by setting the document's page mode to show outlines and marking each outline item as open before saving.
rules: Set {doc}.PageMode = PageMode.UseOutlines to enable the outline (bookmarks) panel when the PDF is opened.
Iterate over each outline item in {doc}.Outlines and set its Open property to true to expand the bookmark hierarchy.
warnings: The example assumes the PDF contains an outline collection; if none exist, the loop has no effect.
OutlineItemCollection is accessed via Document.Outlines, which actually returns an OutlineCollection; the rule abstracts this detail.

semantic_summary: Shows how to export the bookmarks of a PDF document to an XML file using PdfBookmarkEditor and then save the (potentially unchanged) PDF.
rules: Instantiate a PdfBookmarkEditor, then bind the source PDF with BindPdf({input_pdf}) before performing any bookmark operations.
Export the document's bookmarks to an XML file using ExportBookmarksToXML({string_literal}) after the PDF is bound.
Call Save({output_pdf}) on the PdfBookmarkEditor to write out the PDF (required if any modifications are made or to finalize the operation).
warnings: Save() creates a new PDF file even if no bookmark changes were made; it may be unnecessary if only exporting bookmarks.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to extract the hierarchical bookmark list from an existing PDF using the PdfBookmarkEditor facade.
rules: To retrieve bookmarks from a PDF, create a PdfBookmarkEditor, bind it to {input_pdf} with BindPdf, and invoke ExtractBookmarks() to obtain a Bookmarks collection.
Iterate over the returned Bookmarks; each Bookmark exposes Title and PageNumber properties for reading bookmark information.
warnings: The example assumes that {input_pdf} contains at least one bookmark; otherwise the collection will be empty.
Path resolution is performed by an external helper (RunExamples.GetDataDir...), which is not shown in the snippet.

semantic_summary: Shows how to bind a PDF file with PdfBookmarkEditor, extract its bookmarks, and read each bookmark's title, page number and action, including hierarchical level information.
rules: Instantiate a PdfBookmarkEditor and bind the source PDF using BindPdf({input_pdf}) before any bookmark operations.
Invoke ExtractBookmarks() on the bound PdfBookmarkEditor to retrieve a Bookmarks collection representing all document bookmarks.
Iterate over each Bookmark in the collection; access Bookmark.Title, Bookmark.PageNumber, Bookmark.Action, and Bookmark.Level to obtain bookmark metadata and hierarchy.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to open a PDF document with Aspose.PDF, enumerate its top‑level bookmarks (outlines) and read basic bookmark properties such as title, style flags and color.
rules: Load a PDF by creating a new {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});
Iterate over the bookmark collection via foreach (OutlineItemCollection {outline_item} in {doc}.Outlines) to access each bookmark.
Read bookmark attributes like {outline_item}.Title (string), {outline_item}.Italic (bool), {outline_item}.Bold (bool) and {outline_item}.Color (Color) without modifying the document.
warnings: The example only traverses top‑level bookmarks; nested bookmarks require recursive traversal of OutlineItemCollection.SubItems.
The exact type name for the outline collection may be Aspose.Pdf.OutlineCollection, but only OutlineItemCollection is explicitly referenced.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to open a PDF document, enumerate its top‑level bookmarks, and recursively enumerate any child bookmarks while accessing their properties such as title, style, and color.
rules: Load a PDF into a {doc} instance using `new Document({input_pdf})`.
Iterate over top‑level bookmarks with `foreach (OutlineItemCollection {bookmark} in {doc}.Outlines)` and read properties like Title, Italic, Bold, Color.
If `{bookmark}.Count > 0`, iterate its child bookmarks using `foreach (OutlineItemCollection {child} in {bookmark})` and apply the same property accesses.
warnings: The exact type of `Document.Outlines` is assumed to be `OutlineCollection`; if the library version differs, the type name may vary.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to extract all bookmarks from an existing PDF file using the PdfBookmarkEditor class.
rules: Instantiate a PdfBookmarkEditor, call BindPdf({input_pdf}) to load the document before any bookmark operations.
Invoke ExtractBookmarks() on the bound editor to obtain a Bookmarks collection.
Iterate over the Bookmarks collection; each Bookmark exposes Title, PageNumber, and Action properties for reading.
warnings: The example uses the Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace, which may be deprecated in newer Aspose.PDF versions; consider using the main Aspose.Pdf API for future projects.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to import bookmark definitions from an XML file into an existing PDF document using the PdfBookmarkEditor facade.
rules: Bind a PDF file to a PdfBookmarkEditor using BindPdf({input_pdf}) before performing any bookmark operations.
Import bookmarks from an XML file into the bound PDF using ImportBookmarksWithXML({xml_file}).
Persist the updated PDF by calling Save({output_pdf}) on the PdfBookmarkEditor.
warnings: The XML file must follow Aspose's bookmark XML schema; otherwise ImportBookmarksWithXML may throw an exception.
File paths are assumed to be valid and accessible at runtime.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a PDF bookmark that inherits the current zoom level by assigning a GoToAction with an XYZExplicitDestination where the zoom parameter is set to 0.
rules: Create a new OutlineItemCollection passing the document's OutlineCollection ({doc}.Outlines) as the parent, set its Action to a new GoToAction wrapping an XYZExplicitDestination, then add the item to {doc}.Outlines to create a bookmark.

When constructing XYZExplicitDestination, use the signature XYZExplicitDestination({int page}, {float left}, {float top}, {float zoom}); setting {zoom} to 0 causes the bookmark to inherit the viewer's current zoom level.
warnings: The exact fully‑qualified name of OutlineItemCollection's constructor parameter (parent collection) is inferred; if the API differs, adjust accordingly.

semantic_summary: Shows how to load a PDF, modify an existing bookmark's name and title using PdfBookmarkEditor, and save the updated document.
rules: Instantiate {class} (PdfBookmarkEditor), then call BindPdf({input_pdf}) to load the document.
Use ModifyBookmarks({string_literal} oldBookmark, {string_literal} newTitle) to replace the bookmark text.
After modifications, call Save({output_pdf}) to write the changes to a new PDF file.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to open an existing PDF, retrieve a specific bookmark (outline) by index, modify its title and style attributes, and save the updated document.
rules: Load a PDF document from {input_pdf} using `new Document(path)`.
Retrieve a bookmark via `document.Outlines[{int}]`, then set its `Title`, `Italic`, and `Bold` properties, and finally save the document to {output_pdf}.
warnings: The example assumes that an outline exists at the specified zero‑based index; accessing a non‑existent index will throw an exception.
Only the `Title`, `Italic`, and `Bold` properties are shown, but other outline attributes can also be modified.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to open an existing PDF, locate a specific child bookmark, modify its title and style attributes, and save the updated document.
rules: Load a PDF document: var {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});
Access a top‑level bookmark: var {parentBookmark} = {doc}.Outlines[{int}];
Access a child bookmark of the parent: var {childBookmark} = {parentBookmark}[{int}];
Update bookmark properties: {childBookmark}.Title = {string_literal}; {childBookmark}.Italic = {bool}; {childBookmark}.Bold = {bool};
Save the modified PDF: {doc}.Save({output_pdf});
warnings: Outline collections use zero‑based indexing; ensure the specified indices exist.
If the target outline or child does not exist, accessing it will throw an exception.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to concatenate multiple PDF files into a single document using PdfFileEditor, including handling of corrupted source files.
rules: Create a PdfFileEditor instance and call Concatenate({input_pdf_array}, {output_pdf}) to merge the PDFs specified by full file paths.

Set PdfFileEditor.CorruptedFileAction = ConcatenateCorruptedFileAction.ConcatenateIgnoringCorrupted before Concatenate to skip any corrupted PDFs; after concatenation, inspect PdfFileEditor.CorruptedItems for entries with .Index and .Exception.Message.
warnings: The example relies on the Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace; ensure the corresponding assembly is referenced.
RunExamples.GetDataDir_AsposePdfFacades_Pages() is a helper not part of the pattern.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to merge multiple PDF files supplied as streams into a single PDF document using the Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor class.
rules: Instantiate a PdfFileEditor object and call its Concatenate method with an array of input streams ({input_stream_array}) and an output stream ({output_stream}) to produce a merged PDF.
Open each source PDF as a read‑only FileStream (FileMode.Open) and open the destination PDF as a writeable FileStream (FileMode.Create) before invoking Concatenate.
Keep all streams open for the duration of the Concatenate call; dispose or close them after the operation completes.
warnings: The example does not explicitly dispose the FileStream objects; callers should ensure proper disposal to avoid file locks.
Only PDF streams compatible with Aspose.Pdf are supported; encrypted or corrupted PDFs may cause runtime errors.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to concatenate multiple PDF files, including a blank PDF, into a single document by using streams with the Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor class.
rules: Create a PdfFileEditor instance and call its Concatenate method, passing one or more input {input_pdf} streams followed by an output {output_pdf} stream; the order of input streams defines the concatenation order.
Open input PDF streams with FileMode.Open and the output stream with FileMode.Create (or appropriate mode) before invoking Concatenate.
warnings: The example does not dispose the FileStream objects; callers should ensure streams are closed or wrapped in using statements.
File paths are assumed to be valid and files to exist; no error handling is shown.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to merge multiple PDF files into a single document using PdfFileEditor, optionally disabling the copying of outlines (bookmarks).
rules: To concatenate two PDFs, instantiate PdfFileEditor and call Concatenate({input_pdf}, {input_pdf}, {output_pdf}).
To concatenate an arbitrary number of PDFs, provide a string[] of file paths to Concatenate({string_literal[]}, {output_pdf}) and set CopyOutlines = {bool} to control whether bookmarks are preserved.
warnings: Assumes the overload signatures of Concatenate match the shown parameter order; actual library version may differ.
CopyOutlines property is specific to the Facades API and may not affect other concatenation methods.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to merge two PDF files into a single PDF using Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor with stream objects, optionally preserving the logical (tagged) structure of the source PDFs.
rules: Create a PdfFileEditor instance, then call PdfFileEditor.Concatenate({input_stream1}, {input_stream2}, {output_stream}) to merge exactly two PDFs supplied as streams into the output stream.
To retain the logical (tagged) structure of the source PDFs, set PdfFileEditor.CopyLogicalStructure = true before invoking Concatenate; the method returns a bool indicating success.
Always close or dispose the input and output streams after the Concatenate call to release file handles.
warnings: The Concatenate overload used accepts only two input streams; merging more than two PDFs requires repeated calls or a different overload.
The example does not include error handling for I/O exceptions; callers should wrap stream operations in try/finally or using statements.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to merge multiple PDF files, including a blank PDF, into a single output PDF using the PdfFileEditor.Concatenate method.
rules: Use PdfFileEditor.Concatenate({input_pdf}, {input_pdf}, ..., {output_pdf}) to merge an arbitrary number of PDF files into one document.
All file paths are supplied as string literals or variables; the final argument specifies the destination PDF file.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to embed a file as an attachment in a PDF, convert the document to PDF/A‑3a (which preserves attachments), and save the resulting file.
rules: Create a {file_specification} via new FileSpecification({attachment_path}, {description}) and add it to {doc}.EmbeddedFiles to embed an attachment.
When converting to PDF/A‑3, call {doc}.Convert({log_file_path}, PdfFormat.PDF_A_3A, {convert_error_action}) to ensure embedded files are retained.
After conversion, invoke {doc}.Save({output_pdf}) to persist the PDF/A document.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates converting a PDF to HTML while embedding raster images as parts of the page background, avoiding separate image files.
rules: When converting {input_pdf} to HTML, instantiate HtmlSaveOptions, set FixedLayout = true, and set RasterImagesSavingMode = HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingModes.AsEmbeddedPartsOfPngPageBackground to embed images in the page background.
Call {doc}.Save({output_html}, {save_options}) where {save_options} is an HtmlSaveOptions configured as above.

semantic_summary: Shows how to load a CGM file with CgmLoadOptions and convert it to a PDF document using Aspose.PDF.
rules: Load a CGM file by creating a CgmLoadOptions instance and passing it together with the CGM file path to the Document constructor: new Document({input_cgm}, new CgmLoadOptions()).
Save the loaded Document as a PDF by calling Document.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The example assumes the CGM file exists at the specified path and that the Aspose.PDF license (if required) is already configured.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to convert an EPUB file to PDF by loading the EPUB with EpubLoadOptions and saving the resulting Aspose.Pdf.Document as a PDF.
rules: Instantiate an appropriate load options object (e.g., EpubLoadOptions) and pass it to the Document constructor along with the input file path ({string_literal}) to load a non‑PDF source document.
After loading, call Document.Save with the desired output file path ({string_literal}) to generate the PDF.
warnings: The example assumes the presence of a valid Aspose.PDF license; without it, the output may contain a watermark.
Placeholder {string_literal} is used for both input and output file paths; adjust as needed for your environment.

semantic_summary: Shows how to load an SVG file into a PDF document while automatically adjusting the page size to match the SVG dimensions and removing all page margins before saving.
rules: Create a {doc} from an SVG file {string_literal} by passing a SvgLoadOptions instance with AdjustPageSize = {bool} to the Document constructor.
Access a specific page via {doc}.Pages[{int}] and set its PageInfo.Margin.{Top|Left|Bottom|Right} properties to {int} (e.g., 0) to remove default margins.
Save the modified {doc} to a PDF file {string_literal} using Document.Save.
warnings: MarginInfo properties are zero‑based per page; the example uses page index 1 (Aspose.Pdf uses 1‑based indexing for Pages).
AdjustPageSize works only if the SVG contains explicit width/height or viewBox attributes.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert HTML files to PDF using Aspose.Pdf, including custom external resource loading, rendering the whole HTML on a single page, and handling SVG content.
rules: Create HtmlLoadOptions, assign CustomLoaderOfExternalResources = new LoadOptions.ResourceLoadingStrategy({resource_loader}), then instantiate Document({input_html}, options) and call Save({output_pdf}) to convert HTML to PDF with custom resource handling.
Set HtmlLoadOptions.IsRenderToSinglePage = true before loading the HTML to force the entire content to be rendered on a single PDF page.
Initialize HtmlLoadOptions with the base directory of the HTML file (new HtmlLoadOptions(Path.GetDirectoryName({input_html}))) so that relative resource URIs are resolved correctly.
warnings: Nested types LoadOptions.ResourceLoadingStrategy and LoadOptions.ResourceLoadingResult are inferred; exact namespace may differ.
The delegate signature for the custom resource loader is assumed to match (string) => LoadOptions.ResourceLoadingResult.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load a Markdown file into an Aspose.Pdf.Document using MdLoadOptions and then save it as a PDF document.
rules: Create a Document from a Markdown source: Document {doc} = new Document({string_literal}, new MdLoadOptions());
Save the Document to PDF: {doc}.Save({string_literal});
warnings: MdLoadOptions is supported only in Aspose.PDF for .NET version 19.6 or later.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to convert an MHT (MHTML) file to a PDF document using Aspose.PDF for .NET.
rules: Load an MHT file into a Document by invoking the Document constructor with the file path and a new MhtLoadOptions instance: new Document({input_mht}, new MhtLoadOptions()).
Convert the loaded Document to PDF by calling Document.Save with a .pdf file path: document.Save({output_pdf}).

semantic_summary: Demonstrates converting a PDF document to HTML while directing the generated markup to a custom stream via HtmlSaveOptions.CustomHtmlSavingStrategy, and shows how to customize saving of embedded resources and CSS using custom strategies.
rules: To write the HTML result of a PDF conversion to a custom {stream}, create an HtmlSaveOptions instance, set its CustomHtmlSavingStrategy to a HtmlPageMarkupSavingStrategy delegate that receives HtmlPageMarkupSavingInfo, reads htmlSavingInfo.ContentStream and writes the bytes to the desired {stream}.

When a single HTML file is required for the whole PDF, set HtmlSaveOptions.SplitIntoPages = false and configure embedding options such as RasterImagesSavingMode, FontSavingMode, PartsEmbeddingMode, and LettersPositioningMethod to control how images, fonts and layout are embedded.

To control where fonts and images are saved during conversion, assign HtmlSaveOptions.CustomResourceSavingStrategy to a ResourceSavingStrategy delegate that writes resourceSavingInfo.ContentStream to a chosen {resource_folder} and returns a URL (e.g., file:///path) that will be referenced in the generated HTML.

To customize CSS handling, provide HtmlSaveOptions.CustomCssSavingStrategy and CustomStrategyOfCssUrlCreation delegates: the former writes CssSavingInfo.ContentStream to a predefined {css_folder}, the latter returns the URL (e.g., file:///folder/css_style{0}.css) that will be used in the HTML markup.
warnings: Delegate type signatures (HtmlPageMarkupSavingStrategy, ResourceSavingStrategy, CssSavingStrategy, CssUrlMakingStrategy) are inferred from usage; exact namespace-qualified names may differ in older SDK versions.

semantic_summary: Creates a PDF document, adds a page for each JPG file in a folder, determines whether the page should be landscape or portrait by comparing the image width to the page width, sets the page orientation accordingly, inserts the image, and saves the PDF.
rules: For each image file path {string_literal}, create a new page {page} via {doc}.Pages.Add(), load the image with System.Drawing.Bitmap to obtain its Width, compare it to {page}.PageInfo.Width, and set {page}.PageInfo.IsLandscape = true if the image is wider, otherwise false.
Instantiate Aspose.Pdf.Image, set its File property to the image path {string_literal}, and add the image to the page by calling {page}.Paragraphs.Add(image).
After processing all images, persist the PDF by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The example uses System.Drawing.Bitmap which relies on GDI+ and may not be supported in non‑Windows environments.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load a PCL file (from file path or stream) using PclLoadOptions, optionally configure the conversion engine, and save the resulting PDF document.
rules: Load a PCL document by creating a {doc} = new Aspose.Pdf.Document({string_literal} inputPath, new Aspose.Pdf.PclLoadOptions()) and then save it with {doc}.Save({string_literal} outputPath).

When loading PCL from a stream, copy the source stream to a MemoryStream, reset its position, create a PclLoadOptions instance (optionally set ConversionEngine = PclLoadOptions.ConversionEngines.LegacyEngine), then instantiate {doc} = new Aspose.Pdf.Document(memoryStream, pclLoadOptions); after loading you can query {int} pageCount = {doc}.Pages.Count.
warnings: The ConversionEngine property is available only in certain Aspose.PDF versions; ensure the library version supports it.
When using a stream, the stream position must be reset (Seek) before constructing the Document.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates converting a PDF document to Microsoft Word formats (DOC and DOCX) using Aspose.Pdf, including basic conversion and advanced options such as flow mode, proximity tuning, and bullet recognition.
rules: Load a PDF: Document {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});
Basic conversion to DOC: {doc}.Save({string_literal}, SaveFormat.Doc);
Create DocSaveOptions, configure properties (Mode, RelativeHorizontalProximity, RecognizeBullets), then save: {doc}.Save({string_literal}, {save_options});
To produce DOCX, set saveOptions.Format = DocSaveOptions.DocFormat.DocX before calling Save.
warnings: The example uses a helper RunExamples.GetDataDir_AsposePdf_DocumentConversion() for path resolution, which is not part of the Aspose.Pdf API.
Enum values (e.g., DocSaveOptions.RecognitionMode.Flow) are inferred from usage; exact enum names may differ in older library versions.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates converting a PDF file to an ePUB document using Aspose.PDF by loading the PDF, configuring EPUB save options, and saving the result.
rules: Load a PDF document from a file path: `Document {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});`

Instantiate EpubSaveOptions and set the content recognition mode: `EpubSaveOptions {options} = new EpubSaveOptions(); {options}.ContentRecognitionMode = EpubSaveOptions.RecognitionMode.{recognition_mode};`
Save the loaded document as an ePUB file using the configured options: `{doc}.Save({output_epub}, {options});`

semantic_summary: Demonstrates converting a PDF document to HTML using Aspose.PDF with various HtmlSaveOptions to control layout, pagination, resource handling, and styling.
rules: Load a PDF: {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});
Simple conversion: {doc}.Save({output_html}, SaveFormat.Html);

Custom conversion: create HtmlSaveOptions, set desired properties (e.g., {bool} SplitIntoPages, {bool} FixedLayout, {bool} CompressSvgGraphicsIfAny, {string_literal}[] ExcludeFontNameList, {string_literal} DefaultFontName, {enum} PartsEmbeddingMode, {enum} RasterImagesSavingMode, {enum} LettersPositioningMethod), then call {doc}.Save({output_html}, htmlOptions);
Generate multi‑page HTML: set htmlOptions.SplitIntoPages = true before saving.
Control image output: set htmlOptions.SpecialFolderForSvgImages or htmlOptions.SpecialFolderForAllImages to a folder path to store generated SVG or raster images.
Render transparent or shadowed text: set htmlOptions.SaveTransparentTexts = true and/or htmlOptions.SaveShadowedTextsAsTransparentTexts = true.
Preserve PDF layers as HTML layers: set htmlOptions.ConvertMarkedContentToLayers = true.
Create body‑only markup and split pages: set htmlOptions.HtmlMarkupGenerationMode = HtmlSaveOptions.HtmlMarkupGenerationModes.WriteOnlyBodyContent and htmlOptions.SplitIntoPages = true.

Adjust page margins and center alignment: define commonMargin = new SaveOptions.MarginPartStyle({int}); autoMargin = new SaveOptions.MarginPartStyle(true); set htmlOptions.PageMarginIfAny = new HtmlSaveOptions.MarginInfo(commonMargin); then assign htmlOptions.PageMarginIfAny.LeftMarginIfAny = autoMargin and RightMarginIfAny = autoMargin.
warnings: Enum and nested type names are inferred from usage; exact namespace for MarginInfo and MarginPartStyle may differ.
The example uses helper RunExamples.GetDataDir_AsposePdf_DocumentConversion() which is not part of Aspose.PDF API.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load a PDF document, convert it to PDF/A‑1b compliance (with validation) using Aspose.Pdf, and save the resulting file.
rules: Load a PDF: var {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});
Convert to PDF/A: {doc}.Convert({log_path}, PdfFormat.PDF_A_1B, ConvertErrorAction.Delete); // validation performed during conversion
Save the converted document: {doc}.Save({output_pdf});
warnings: The example uses a helper RunExamples.GetDataDir_AsposePdf_DocumentConversion() for path resolution, which is not part of Aspose.Pdf API.
ConvertErrorAction determines how validation errors are handled; other actions (e.g., Throw) are also available.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load an existing PDF, convert it to PDF/A‑3B compliance using Aspose.Pdf, and save the resulting document.
rules: Load a PDF into a {doc} by constructing Aspose.Pdf.Document with the path to {input_pdf}.

Convert {doc} to a specific PDF/A compliance level by calling {doc}.Convert(outputStream, PdfFormat.PDF_A_3B, ConvertErrorAction.{action}), where {action} defines how conversion errors are handled (e.g., Delete).
After conversion, persist the updated {doc} to a file using {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to convert an existing PDF file to a PDF/X‑4 compliant document by configuring conversion options such as target format, error handling, ICC profile, and output intent, then saving the result.
rules: Load {input_pdf} into a new Aspose.Pdf.Document instance.
Create a PdfFormatConversionOptions object with target format {PdfFormat.PDF_X_4} and error action {ConvertErrorAction.Delete}.
Optionally assign an ICC profile file path to options.IccProfileFileName ({string_literal}).
Optionally set options.OutputIntent to a new OutputIntent with an identifier ({string_literal}).
Invoke {doc}.Convert(options) to perform the format conversion.
Save the converted document to {output_pdf} using {doc}.Save.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to convert each page of a PDF document to PNG images while enabling font hinting for improved text rendering.
rules: Load {input_pdf} into a Document {doc}; create a RenderingOptions {opts} with UseFontHinting = true; create a Resolution {resolution} (e.g., 300 DPI); instantiate a PngDevice with {resolution}; assign {opts} to device.RenderingOptions; iterate over {doc}.Pages and for each {page} call device.Process({page}, output_stream).
When saving each page as PNG, construct the output file path using the page index (e.g., "image{page}_out.png") and write the image via a FileStream opened with FileMode.Create.
warnings: The exact namespace for RenderingOptions is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.RenderingOptions; if the library version differs, the type name may vary.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF document to PPTX using Aspose.PDF, with options to render slides as images and to track conversion progress via a custom progress handler.
rules: Load a PDF file into a {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document) and call {doc}.Save({output_pptx}, new PptxSaveOptions()) to generate a PPTX presentation.
Set PptxSaveOptions.SlidesAsImages = true before saving to export each slide as an image instead of editable shapes.

Assign a method matching PptxSaveOptions.ProgressEventHandlerInfo to PptxSaveOptions.CustomProgressHandler to receive ProgressEventType events (TotalProgress, SourcePageAnalysed, ResultPageCreated, ResultPageSaved) for monitoring conversion progress.
warnings: The example relies on a helper (RunExamples.GetDataDir_AsposePdf_DocumentConversion) for path resolution, which is not part of the core conversion logic.
CustomProgressHandler requires a delegate with the exact signature void (PptxSaveOptions.ProgressEventHandlerInfo); ensure proper using directives are included.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF document to SVG format using Aspose.PDF, with optional control over ZIP compression of the output.
rules: Load a PDF by creating a {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});
Create a {svg_options} = new SvgSaveOptions(); optionally set {svg_options}.CompressOutputToZipArchive = {bool};
Save the document as SVG using {doc}.Save({output_svg}, {svg_options});

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF document to LaTeX (TeX) format using Aspose.PDF by configuring TeXSaveOptions and invoking Document.Save.
rules: Load a PDF file into a {doc} instance via the Document(string {input_pdf}) constructor.
Create a TeXSaveOptions object, optionally set its OutDirectoryPath to {output_directory}, and pass it to {doc}.Save(string {output_tex}, TeXSaveOptions) to produce a LaTeX file.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates converting a PDF document to Excel formats (XLS and XLSX) using Aspose.Pdf, including how to configure output options such as file format, column insertion, and worksheet consolidation.
rules: Load a PDF file {input_pdf} into an Aspose.Pdf.Document instance and call Document.Save({output_excel}, ExcelSaveOptions) to export to Excel.
Set ExcelSaveOptions.Format = ExcelSaveOptions.ExcelFormat.XLSX to generate an .xlsx file instead of the default .xls.
Set ExcelSaveOptions.InsertBlankColumnAtFirst = {bool} to control whether a blank column is added at the beginning of each worksheet.
Set ExcelSaveOptions.MinimizeTheNumberOfWorksheets = {bool} to combine all PDF pages into a single worksheet when true.
warnings: The example assumes the presence of an input PDF file at the specified path.
Output file paths are hard‑coded; in production code they should be configurable.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load a PDF document and export it to an XML representation using the MobiXml save format.
rules: Load a PDF file into a Document object: new Document({input_pdf})
Save the Document as XML using the MobiXml format: {doc}.Save({output_xml}, SaveFormat.MobiXml)

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF file to an XPS document by loading the PDF with Aspose.Pdf.Document, creating default XpsSaveOptions, and saving the result using Document.Save.
rules: Load a PDF: var {doc} = new Document("{input_pdf}");
Instantiate XPS save options: var {save_options} = new XpsSaveOptions();
Save the document as XPS: {doc}.Save("{output_xps}", {save_options});

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to convert a PDF/A compliant document to a regular PDF by removing the PDF/A compliance information and saving the result.
rules: Load a PDF/A file into a {doc} using the Document constructor with {input_pdf}.
Call {doc}.RemovePdfaCompliance() to strip PDF/A compliance metadata.
Save the modified {doc} to a regular PDF using {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PostScript (.ps) file to a PDF document by using PsLoadOptions when loading the source file and then saving the resulting Document as PDF.
rules: Instantiate a PsLoadOptions object and pass it to the Document constructor together with the path to a .ps file to load PostScript content.
After loading, call Document.Save with a target file path (PDF extension) to write the converted PDF.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates converting a PDF document to HTML while applying a custom prefix to the generated CSS class names using HtmlSaveOptions.
rules: Load a PDF into a {doc} by constructing Aspose.Pdf.Document with the path to {input_pdf}.

Create an Aspose.Pdf.HtmlSaveOptions instance, set its CssClassNamesPrefix property to a {string_literal} that will be prefixed to all CSS class names in the output HTML, and pass this options object to {doc}.Save(outputPath, saveOptions) to produce the HTML file.
warnings: The CssClassNamesPrefix property is only applicable when saving to HTML formats; using it with other save formats has no effect.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to use a custom resource‑saving strategy with HtmlSaveOptions to control font file naming and URL generation when converting a PDF to HTML.
rules: When converting {input_pdf} to HTML, create an HtmlSaveOptions instance and assign its CustomResourceSavingStrategy property to a method that receives SaveOptions.ResourceSavingInfo.

In the custom method, if resourceSavingInfo.ResourceType != SaveOptions.NodeLevelResourceType.Font, set resourceSavingInfo.CustomProcessingCancelled = true and return an empty string to let the default converter handle the resource.

For Font resources, generate a unique short file name (e.g., {int}{original_extension}), write the font bytes from resourceSavingInfo.ContentStream to a desired folder, and return a custom URI (e.g., "http://localhost/.../{shortFontFileName}") that will be referenced in the generated HTML.
warnings: The example uses a hard‑coded localhost URL and Windows‑style path separators; adapt these for your environment.
The delegate type HtmlSaveOptions.ResourceSavingStrategy may have a different signature in newer Aspose.PDF versions.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF to HTML while extracting raster images as external PNG files referenced via SVG and how to supply a custom resource‑saving strategy that writes images to a specific folder and returns custom URL prefixes for the generated HTML.
rules: When converting {doc} to HTML, create an HtmlSaveOptions instance, set options.RasterImagesSavingMode = HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingModes.AsExternalPngFilesReferencedViaSvg, and pass the options to Document.Save(output_html, options).

Define a custom resource saving strategy via options.CustomResourceSavingStrategy = new HtmlSaveOptions.ResourceSavingStrategy(Method) where Method receives a SaveOptions.ResourceSavingInfo; if the info is not HtmlSaveOptions.HtmlImageSavingInfo, set resourceSavingInfo.CustomProcessingCancelled = true and return an empty string, otherwise write the image bytes to a desired {output_folder} and return a custom URL (e.g., "http://localhost/.../{resourceSavingInfo.SupposedFileName}") to be used in the generated HTML/SVG.
warnings: The sample writes image bytes using File.WriteAllBytes(dataDir, ...) which appears to be a mistake; the intended path should likely be outPath.
The custom URL strings contain spaces ("http:// Localhost:255/"), which would produce invalid URLs.
The delegate type HtmlSaveOptions.ResourceSavingStrategy is inferred; if the actual signature differs, the rule may need adjustment.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to customize CSS URL generation and CSS file saving when converting a PDF to HTML by assigning custom delegate strategies to HtmlSaveOptions.
rules: When converting {input_pdf} to HTML, create an HtmlSaveOptions instance and set HtmlSaveOptions.CustomStrategyOfCssUrlCreation to a delegate that returns a URL template (e.g., "http://localhost/.../style{0}.css") for CSS resources.

Assign HtmlSaveOptions.CustomCssSavingStrategy to a delegate that receives HtmlSaveOptions.CssSavingInfo, extracts the CSS file name from CssSavingInfo.SupposedURL using the known template, builds a full path (e.g., {folder}\style{extracted}.css), and writes the content stream to that path.
Call {doc}.Save({output_html}, saveOptions) with the configured HtmlSaveOptions to apply the custom CSS handling during PDF‑to‑HTML conversion.
warnings: The custom CSS saving logic assumes a specific URL template format and uses string manipulation based on a GUID placeholder; changes to the template require updating the extraction code.
The example stores the output folder in a static field (_folderForReferencedResources_36435), which may not be thread‑safe in concurrent scenarios.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF to a single HTML file while receiving detailed conversion progress events via a custom progress handler.
rules: Assign a method matching HtmlSaveOptions.ConversionProgessEventHandler to HtmlSaveOptions.CustomProgressHandler; the method receives HtmlSaveOptions.ProgressEventHandlerInfo and can switch on eventInfo.EventType (TotalProgress, SourcePageAnalysed, ResultPageCreated, ResultPageSaved) to report progress.
Set HtmlSaveOptions.SplitIntoPages = {bool} to control whether the HTML output is generated as a single file (false) or split per source PDF page (true).
Load a PDF with new Document({input_pdf}) and invoke doc.Save({output_html}, {save_options}) where {save_options} is an instance of HtmlSaveOptions configured as needed.
warnings: The example comments out the assignment to CustomProgressHandler; the rule assumes the line is uncommented for the progress reporting to work.
License initialization is shown but omitted from the rule set; ensure a valid license is set before conversion.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to convert HTML content that requires authentication into a PDF by supplying credentials both for the initial HTTP request and for external resources referenced in the HTML.
rules: When loading HTML that references external resources requiring authentication, set HtmlLoadOptions.ExternalResourcesCredentials to an ICredentials implementation before creating the Document.

Retrieve protected HTML via WebRequest (or any HTTP client) with the required Credentials, read the response into a string, wrap it in a MemoryStream, and pass the stream together with the configured HtmlLoadOptions to the Document constructor.
warnings: The example uses placeholder URLs with spaces ("http:// My.signchart.com/...") which are not valid URLs.
DefaultCredentials are used; in real scenarios appropriate credentials must be supplied.
Streams are not disposed via using statements, which may lead to resource leaks.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load an HTML file into a PDF, locate link annotations, clear their URI actions, adjust the visual appearance of the linked text, and remove the annotations before saving the result.
rules: Load an HTML file into a {doc} using new HtmlLoadOptions().
Iterate over each {annotation} in {doc}.Pages[1].Annotations and filter where {annotation}.AnnotationType == AnnotationType.Link.
Cast the filtered annotation to LinkAnnotation ({link_annotation}) and check if {link_annotation}.Action is GoToURIAction.
If the action is GoToURIAction, set its URI property to an empty string to disable the hyperlink.

Create a TextFragmentAbsorber with TextSearchOptions limited to {link_annotation}.Rect, accept it on the corresponding page, and for each {text_fragment} set Underline = false and ForegroundColor = Color.Black to remove visual link styling.
Delete the original {link_annotation} from its page using {page}.Annotations.Delete({annotation}).
Save the modified {doc} to the desired output PDF file.
warnings: Aspose.Pdf pages are 1‑based; ensure correct page index when accessing doc.Pages.
Modifying the Annotations collection while iterating may cause runtime issues in some environments; consider iterating over a copy or collecting items to delete first.

semantic_summary: Shows how to substitute a missing font with an available one, then convert a PDF to PDF/A format while handling conversion errors, and finally save the resulting document.
rules: If FontRepository.FindFont({font_missing_name}) throws, add a substitution via FontRepository.Substitutions.Add(new SimpleFontSubstitution({font_missing_name}, {font_substitute_name})) before processing the document.

To convert {input_pdf} to a PDF/A version, call Document.Convert({output_path}, {pdf_a_version}, {error_action}) where {pdf_a_version} is a PdfFormat enum value (e.g., PdfFormat.PDF_A_1B) and {error_action} is a ConvertErrorAction enum value (e.g., ConvertErrorAction.Delete).
After conversion, persist the changes with Document.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The substitution is added to the global FontRepository.Substitutions collection, affecting all subsequent PDF operations in the same AppDomain.
The example assumes the substitute font (e.g., "Arial") is installed on the target machine.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF document to HTML while controlling how fonts are saved (TTF, WOFF, or all formats) and preserving the original layout.
rules: Create a {doc} from {input_pdf}, instantiate HtmlSaveOptions as {save_options}, set {save_options}.FixedLayout = true to keep the original layout, then configure {save_options}.FontSavingMode to one of HtmlSaveOptions.FontSavingModes (AlwaysSaveAsTTF, AlwaysSaveAsWOFF, SaveInAllFormats) before calling {doc}.Save({output_html}, {save_options}).
When a single HTML file is required, set {save_options}.SplitIntoPages = false; otherwise omit or set to true for page‑wise output.
If external raster images are desired, set {save_options}.RasterImagesSavingMode to HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingModes.AsExternalPngFilesReferencedViaSvg before saving.
warnings: The example uses a helper RunExamples.GetDataDir_* to locate files; replace with actual paths in real code.
HtmlSaveOptions.FontSavingModes enum values may vary between Aspose.PDF versions; ensure the used version supports the selected mode.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates converting a PDF to HTML while using custom saving strategies to intercept and control how HTML markup, CSS, fonts, and images are persisted, allowing split‑by‑page output and custom resource URIs.
rules: When converting PDF to HTML, set HtmlSaveOptions.SplitIntoPages = true to generate separate HTML files per PDF page.

Assign custom delegates to HtmlSaveOptions.CustomHtmlSavingStrategy, CustomResourceSavingStrategy, CustomStrategyOfCssUrlCreation, and CustomCssSavingStrategy to intercept the saving of HTML markup, resources (fonts/images), CSS URLs, and CSS content respectively.

In a custom resource saving delegate, inspect SaveOptions.ResourceSavingInfo.ResourceType to differentiate between Font and Image resources, process the stream as needed, and return a URI (e.g., the SupposedFileName) that will be referenced in the generated CSS or HTML.

Configure HtmlSaveOptions.FontSavingMode = HtmlSaveOptions.FontSavingModes.SaveInAllFormats and HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingMode = HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingModes.AsEmbeddedPartsOfPngPageBackground to control how fonts and raster images are embedded or saved.
warnings: The example uses placeholder paths; callers must ensure the custom strategies write to a valid storage location.
Custom delegates must return a valid URI string; otherwise the generated HTML/CSS may reference non‑existent resources.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates converting a PDF to a single HTML file while preserving the original layout and saving raster images as external PNG files referenced via SVG.
rules: Load a PDF document using new Document({input_pdf}) and store it in {doc}.

Create HtmlSaveOptions, set FixedLayout = {bool}, SplitIntoPages = {bool}, and RasterImagesSavingMode = HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingModes.{enum_value} to control image handling, then call {doc}.Save({output_html}, saveOptions).
warnings: The example assumes the output directory exists and is writable.
The specific enum value AsExternalPngFilesReferencedViaSvg requires a recent version of Aspose.PDF.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to specify a default fallback font when rendering PDF pages to PNG images using RenderingOptions with a PngDevice.
rules: Create a RenderingOptions object, set its DefaultFontName = {string_literal}, and assign it to the image device's RenderingOptions before calling Process.
Instantiate a PngDevice (or other image device) with a Resolution of {int} DPI, then call device.Process({page} of {doc}, {output_image_stream}) to render the page to an image.
warnings: Requires Aspose.PDF version 19.6 or later for the DefaultFontName property.
The specified font must be installed or accessible on the system where the code runs.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to change the page size and alignment of a PDF, scale its content, and then convert the resized document to HTML with a visible page border.
rules: To resize pages, create a PdfPageEditor, bind the source PDF via BindPdf({input_pdf}), set PageSize = new PageSize({float} width, {float} height), optionally set VerticalAlignmentType = VerticalAlignment.{enum_value} and HorizontalAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.{enum_value}, and adjust Zoom to a scaling factor calculated from the original page dimensions.

After editing, persist the changes to a MemoryStream with PdfPageEditor.Save({stream}), then instantiate a new Document from that stream ({doc} = new Document({stream})) for further processing such as format conversion.

When converting a Document to HTML, configure HtmlSaveOptions, create a BorderPartStyle (LineType = HtmlBorderLineType.{enum_value}, Color = {color}), assign it to SaveOptions.BorderInfo, set HtmlSaveOptions.PageBorderIfAny = new BorderInfo(borderStyle), and save via Document.Save({string_literal} outputPath, htmlOptions).
warnings: The example uses System.Drawing.Color which may not be supported in all .NET runtimes (e.g., .NET Core on non‑Windows platforms).
Zoom is calculated using only the first page's dimensions; multi‑page PDFs with varying sizes may require per‑page handling.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF document to a single self‑contained HTML file by embedding all resources (images, fonts, CSS) using Aspose.Pdf's HtmlSaveOptions.
rules: Load a PDF from {input_pdf} using `new Document({input_pdf})`.
Instantiate `HtmlSaveOptions` and set `PartsEmbeddingMode = HtmlSaveOptions.PartsEmbeddingModes.EmbedAllIntoHtml` to embed all resources into the HTML output.

Optionally adjust rendering settings: `LettersPositioningMethod = HtmlSaveOptions.LettersPositioningMethods.UseEmUnitsAndCompensationOfRoundingErrorsInCss`, `RasterImagesSavingMode = HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingModes.AsEmbeddedPartsOfPngPageBackground`, `FontSavingMode = HtmlSaveOptions.FontSavingModes.SaveInAllFormats`.
Save the document to a single HTML file using `doc.Save({output_html}, htmlOptions)`.
warnings: The `LettersPositioningMethod` setting is primarily for IE compatibility and may be omitted for other browsers.
Output path is relative; ensure the working directory is writable or provide an absolute path.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF to HTML while providing a custom resource‑saving strategy that intercepts image resources, saves only SVG images to a specific folder, and injects a custom URL for those images in the generated HTML.
rules: When converting {input_pdf} to HTML, instantiate HtmlSaveOptions, optionally set SplitIntoPages = {bool}, assign CustomResourceSavingStrategy = new HtmlSaveOptions.ResourceSavingStrategy({delegate_method}), and call {doc}.Save({output_html}, saveOptions).

Inside the custom delegate ({delegate_method}), if the provided ResourceSavingInfo is not an HtmlSaveOptions.HtmlImageSavingInfo, set resourceSavingInfo.CustomProcessingCancelled = true and return "" to let the default converter handle the resource.

If the resource is an HtmlImageSavingInfo but its ImageType is not Svg or ZippedSvg, also set CustomProcessingCancelled = true and return ""; otherwise, write resourceSavingInfo.ContentStream to a desired folder and return a custom URL string that will be used as the image src in the generated HTML.
warnings: The delegate must match the signature (SaveOptions.ResourceSavingInfo) => string; ensure the returned string is a valid URL or relative path for the HTML.
Custom processing cancels the built‑in image handling; forgetting to set CustomProcessingCancelled may cause duplicate resources.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a PDF document to HTML with each page rendered separately and CSS split into per‑page files, using custom saving and URL‑creation strategies.
rules: When converting {doc} to HTML, enable pagination by setting HtmlSaveOptions.SplitIntoPages = true.

To generate separate CSS files per HTML page, set HtmlSaveOptions.SplitCssIntoPages = true and assign HtmlSaveOptions.CustomCssSavingStrategy to a method that receives HtmlSaveOptions.CssSavingInfo (containing CssNumber and ContentStream) and writes the stream to a file path that incorporates the {int} page number.

To control the href attribute for each generated CSS file, assign HtmlSaveOptions.CustomStrategyOfCssUrlCreation to a method that receives HtmlSaveOptions.CssUrlRequestInfo and returns a string URL containing a placeholder (e.g., "{0}") for the CSS page number.
Optionally, set HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingMode = HtmlSaveOptions.RasterImagesSavingModes.AsPngImagesEmbeddedIntoSvg to preserve legacy image handling behavior.
warnings: Custom delegate signatures must match the expected Aspose.Pdf.HtmlSaveOptions.CssSavingStrategy and CssUrlMakingStrategy signatures; mismatched parameters will cause runtime errors.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a PDF document, add a FloatingBox container, and insert Heading objects with custom numbering styles (Roman numerals, lowercase letters) and automatic sequencing.
rules: To set up a PDF with specific dimensions and margins: instantiate {doc}=Aspose.Pdf.Document, assign {doc}.PageInfo.Width, {doc}.PageInfo.Height, and {doc}.PageInfo.Margin (using Aspose.Pdf.MarginInfo), then add a {page}=Aspose.Pdf.Page via {doc}.Pages.Add() and repeat the size/margin settings on {page}.PageInfo.

To host headings inside a floating container: create {floating_box}=Aspose.Pdf.FloatingBox, set its Margin to match the page margins, add it to {page}.Paragraphs, then add Heading objects to {floating_box}.Paragraphs.

To create a numbered heading: instantiate {heading}=Aspose.Pdf.Heading(level), set IsInList = true, optionally set StartNumber = {int}, assign Style = Aspose.Pdf.NumberingStyle.{enum_value}, enable IsAutoSequence = true, set Text = {string_literal}, and add the heading to a paragraph collection.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to embed raster images into a PDF both by using low‑level graphics operators for precise placement and by using the high‑level Image class for DICOM images.
rules: To place an image at a specific {rect} on a {page}: add the image stream to {page}.Resources.Images, then add a GSave operator, a ConcatenateMatrix operator built from the rectangle dimensions, a Do operator referencing the newly added XImage name, and finally a GRestore operator.
To embed a DICOM image: create an Aspose.Pdf.Image, set its FileType to ImageFileType.Dicom, assign the DICOM file stream to ImageStream, and add the image to {page}.Paragraphs.
warnings: The matrix calculation assumes user space coordinates; callers must ensure the rectangle values are in the same units as the PDF page.
The image stream must remain open (or be fully read) until the document is saved; otherwise the image may not be rendered.

Call Save({output_pdf}) to write the changes and then Close() to release resources.
warnings: AddImage expects coordinates in points; ensure correct conversion if using other units.

Apply Bradley binarization on an existing TIFF stream using TiffDevice.BinarizeBradley(inputStream, outputStream, {float}) where the float threshold is between 0 and 1.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to generate JPEG thumbnail images for every page of each PDF file in a folder using Aspose.PDF's JpegDevice and Resolution classes.

For each page, create a `Resolution` instance (`new Resolution({int dpi})`) and a `JpegDevice` (`new JpegDevice({int width}, {int height}, {resolution}, {int quality})`). Then call `jpegDevice.Process(Document.Pages[{page}], {output_stream})` to render the page to a JPEG image.
Write the rendered image to a file by opening a `FileStream` with `FileMode.Create` pointing to a path such as `{output_dir}\Thumbanils{counter}_{page}.jpg` and passing that stream to `Process`.

Save the retrieved {XImage} to a file stream using XImage.Save(stream, ImageFormat.{string_literal}) where ImageFormat matches the desired output format.
After image extraction, the original {doc} can be saved to {output_pdf} with Document.Save(path) even if no modifications were made.

The example saves the PDF without changes; this step is optional.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to extract raster images from a specific page of a PDF using Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfExtractor and save them to disk.

Call ExtractImage() after configuring page range, then loop while HasNextImage() and retrieve each image with GetNextImage({output_stream}) for further processing or saving.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to extract all images from a PDF document into memory streams using PdfExtractor and optionally save them to disk.

Iterate over extracted images using while (pdfExtractor.HasNextImage()) and retrieve each image into a stream with GetNextImage({output_stream}).
