semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to quickly shrink/compress images in an existing PDF by using Aspose.PDF's fast image compression option and saving the optimized document.
rules: Create {doc} = new Document({input_pdf}); then instantiate {opt} = new OptimizationOptions(); set {opt}.ImageCompressionOptions.CompressImages = {bool}; set {opt}.ImageCompressionOptions.ImageQuality = {int}; set {opt}.ImageCompressionOptions.Version = ImageCompressionVersion.Fast; finally call {doc}.OptimizeResources({opt});
Persist the optimized PDF by invoking {doc}.Save({output_pdf});
warnings: The exact fully qualified name of ImageCompressionOptions is inferred as Aspose.Pdf.Optimization.ImageCompressionOptions; adjust if different.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to apply Flate (ZIP) compression to all images in a PDF by configuring OptimizationOptions and invoking OptimizeResources.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document) from {input_pdf}, create an {optimization_options} (Aspose.Pdf.Optimization.OptimizationOptions), set {optimization_options}.ImageCompressionOptions.Encoding = ImageEncoding.Flate, then call {doc}.OptimizeResources({optimization_options}) to recompress all embedded images using Flate.
After resource optimization, persist the changes by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to enumerate all images on each page of a PDF, retrieve each image's color type, and count grayscale versus RGB images using Aspose.PDF.
rules: Load a PDF into a {doc} via new Document({input_pdf}).
For each {page} in {doc}.Pages, instantiate an ImagePlacementAbsorber, then call {page}.Accept(absorber) to collect all ImagePlacement objects on that page.
Iterate over absorber.ImagePlacements; for each ImagePlacement {imagePlacement}, obtain its Image via {imagePlacement}.Image and call GetColorType() to get a {color} (ColorType enum).
Use a switch or conditional on the returned {color} to apply logic (e.g., counting grayscale vs RGB images).
warnings: GetColorType() may require the image to be fully loaded; large PDFs with many images could impact performance.
ImagePlacementAbsorber only captures images that are placed on the page layout, not images embedded in form fields or annotations.

semantic_summary: Shows how to enumerate image objects on a PDF page, track the current transformation matrix via GSave/GRestore and ConcatenateMatrix operators, and compute each image's displayed size and effective resolution.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} and select a page {page} (e.g., {doc}.Pages[1]); initialize a Stack of System.Drawing.Drawing2D.Matrix with an identity matrix and push it before processing operators.

Iterate over each Operator in {page}.Contents; when the operator is a GSave, push a clone of the current matrix; when it is a GRestore, pop the matrix; when it is a ConcatenateMatrix, multiply the current matrix by the operator's matrix to update the CTM.

When encountering a Do operator whose Name exists in {page}.Resources.Images.Names, retrieve the XImage via {page}.Resources.Images[operator.Name]; use the current CTM (top of the stack) to compute displayed width = sqrt(CTM.Elements[0]^2 + CTM.Elements[1]^2) and height = sqrt(CTM.Elements[2]^2 + CTM.Elements[3]^2); calculate effective resolution as (image.Width * {int defaultResolution}) / displayed width and similarly for height.
warnings: The example assumes 1‑based page indexing and processes only the first page.
It uses non‑generic System.Collections.Stack, which may be replaced by a generic Stack<Matrix> in newer code.
Resolution calculation assumes uniform scaling and does not account for rotation or skew transformations.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to extract image placements from a specific PDF page, read their geometric and resolution properties, and retrieve the embedded image data as a bitmap with its displayed dimensions.
rules: To extract image placements from a given page, instantiate {abs} = new ImagePlacementAbsorber(); then invoke {doc}.Pages[{int}].Accept({abs}); iterate over {abs}.ImagePlacements.
For each {imagePlacement} in the absorber, access placement geometry via {imagePlacement}.Rectangle (Width, Height, LLX, LLY) and resolution via {imagePlacement}.Resolution (X, Y).

To obtain the original image stream, call {imagePlacement}.Image.Save({stream}, ImageFormat.Png); then create a Bitmap from the stream. Optionally resize the bitmap to {imagePlacement}.Rectangle.Width/Height to match the displayed size.
warnings: Assumed Rectangle type is Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle; if a different namespace is used, adjust accordingly.
Resolution property type not explicitly verified; assumed to expose X and Y components.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert an image file into a PDF by creating a document, adding a page with zero margins, loading the image into a stream, inserting it as an Aspose.Pdf.Image, and saving the result.
rules: Create a {doc}, add a {page} via {doc}.Pages.Add(), and set all {page}.PageInfo.Margin.{Top,Bottom,Left,Right} to 0 to remove default margins.

Load image bytes into a {stream}, instantiate an Aspose.Pdf.Image, assign its ImageStream property to the {stream}, optionally set {page}.CropBox = new Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle(0,0,{width},{height}) to match image size, add the image to {page}.Paragraphs, then save {doc} to {output_pdf}.
warnings: The example uses System.Drawing.Bitmap to obtain image dimensions, which is outside the Aspose.PDF API.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to convert a large CGM vector image into a PDF document using Aspose.PDF's PdfProducer, optionally configuring page dimensions via CgmImportOptions.
rules: Create a CgmImportOptions instance and set its PageSize property to a SizeF({float}, {float}) to define the target page dimensions for CGM import.
Invoke PdfProducer.Produce({input_file}, ImportFormat.Cgm, {output_file}) to convert the CGM file to a PDF.
warnings: The CgmImportOptions object is instantiated but not passed to PdfProducer.Produce, so the PageSize setting may have no effect in this example.

semantic_summary: Shows how to render a specific page of a PDF document to an EMF image file using a custom resolution and canvas size.
rules: Load a PDF document from {input_pdf} into a {doc} instance, then select a target {page} via doc.Pages[index].

Create a {Resolution} with desired DPI ({int}), instantiate an {EmfDevice} with width ({int}), height ({int}), and the resolution, then call emfDevice.Process({page}, {output_stream}) to generate an EMF image.
warnings: The width and height parameters for EmfDevice are expressed in points (1/72 inch); ensure they match the intended output size.
The example does not explicitly dispose the EmfDevice; although not required, disposing may be advisable in long‑running applications.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to render a specific page of a PDF document to a PNG image using a custom resolution.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document) and access the desired {page} via {doc}.Pages[{int}].
Create a {resolution} (Aspose.Pdf.Devices.Resolution) with the required DPI, instantiate a PngDevice with that resolution, and call PngDevice.Process({page}, {output_stream}) to write the PNG.
warnings: Aspose.Pdf uses 1‑based indexing for pages; ensure the page number matches this convention.
The example relies on a FileStream for output; callers must manage stream disposal if not using a using block.

semantic_summary: Shows how to convert a single page of a PDF document to a TIFF image using a custom resolution and TiffSettings such as compression, color depth, orientation, and blank‑page handling.
rules: Create a Resolution object with the desired DPI ({int}) and configure a TiffSettings object (Compression, Depth, Shape, SkipBlankPages) before constructing a TiffDevice.
Invoke TiffDevice.Process({doc}, {int startPage}, {int endPage}, {string_literal outputPath}) to render the specified page range of the PDF to a TIFF file.
warnings: Enum types (CompressionType, ColorDepth, ShapeType) are assumed to be in the Aspose.Pdf.Devices namespace; if the library version differs, fully qualified names may vary.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to convert each page of a PDF document, or a specific page, into JPEG images using Aspose.Pdf's JpegDevice with configurable resolution and quality.
rules: Iterate over {doc}.Pages (1‑based index) and for each {page} create a FileStream for the output image, instantiate a JpegDevice with a Resolution object (e.g., new Resolution({int} dpi)) and a quality value (0‑100), then call JpegDevice.Process({page}, stream) to render the page as a JPEG.
To render a single page, directly access {doc}.Pages[{int pageNumber}] and pass it to JpegDevice.Process with a prepared output stream.

When constructing JpegDevice, use the overload JpegDevice(Resolution resolution, int quality) where quality must be within 0‑100; higher values yield better image quality at the cost of larger file size.
warnings: The example uses the JpegDevice overload without explicit width/height; the output image size is derived from the PDF page dimensions and the specified resolution.
FileStream is manually closed after processing; using a 'using' block already disposes the stream, so explicit Close() is redundant.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to replace an existing image in a PDF page by loading a new image stream and saving the modified document.
rules: Load a PDF document from {input_pdf} using new Document(path).
Replace an image on {page} by invoking {doc}.Pages[{int}].Resources.Images.Replace({int}, {image_stream}) and then save the document to {output_pdf}.
warnings: The example assumes the target image index exists; otherwise Replace will throw.
Page and image indexes are 1‑based in Aspose.PDF, which may differ from zero‑based conventions.

semantic_summary: Shows how to replace an existing image on a specific page of a PDF using the PdfContentEditor class from Aspose.PDF.
rules: Bind an existing PDF to a PdfContentEditor instance using BindPdf({input_pdf}).
Replace an image on a given page by calling ReplaceImage({page}, {image_index}, {image_path}) where page numbers are 1‑based and image_index identifies the image position on that page.
Persist the changes by invoking Save({output_pdf}) on the same PdfContentEditor instance.
warnings: The meaning of {image_index} (e.g., whether it is zero‑based or one‑based) is inferred from the example; actual behavior should be verified against documentation.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to compress and resize images within a PDF by configuring OptimizationOptions and applying them to a Document.
rules: Load {input_pdf} into a Document, create a new Aspose.Pdf.Optimization.OptimizationOptions instance, enable image compression with ImageCompressionOptions.CompressImages = {bool}, set ImageCompressionOptions.ImageQuality = {int}, enable resizing with ImageCompressionOptions.ResizeImages = {bool}, and optionally limit resolution via ImageCompressionOptions.MaxResolution = {int}, then call Document.OptimizeResources with the configured options.
After optimization, persist the changes by calling Document.Save with {output_pdf}.
warnings: Exact fully qualified name of ImageCompressionOptions inferred as Aspose.Pdf.Optimization.ImageCompressionOptions; verify against library version.
Namespace of OptimizationOptions assumed as Aspose.Pdf.Optimization; adjust if library structure differs.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to locate every image placed in a PDF, retrieve the underlying XImage object, and read placement metadata such as rectangle bounds and resolution using an ImagePlacementAbsorber.
rules: Create an ImagePlacementAbsorber ({abs}) and apply it to all pages of a Document ({doc}) via doc.Pages.Accept({abs}) to collect image placement information.

Iterate over {abs}.ImagePlacements; each ImagePlacement provides the embedded XImage via ImagePlacement.Image and placement details via ImagePlacement.Rectangle (Width, Height, LLX, LLY) and ImagePlacement.Resolution (X, Y).

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add an image to a PDF page with explicit width and height, set the page size, and save the resulting document.
rules: Create an {image} instance, set {image}.FixWidth = {int} and {image}.FixHeight = {int} (points), assign {image}.File = {string_literal}, optionally set {image}.FileType = {enum_literal}, then add the image to {page}.Paragraphs.
Adjust page dimensions by setting {page}.PageInfo.Width = {int} and {page}.PageInfo.Height = {int} (points) before saving.
Persist the PDF by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: FixWidth and FixHeight are measured in points (1 point = 1/72 inch).
Using ImageFileType.Unknown works for most raster formats but may be unnecessary; specific types (e.g., ImageFileType.Jpeg) can be used.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to reduce the size of images in an existing PDF by applying compression and quality settings using Aspose.PDF's optimization API.
rules: Load a PDF document from {input_pdf} using new Document(string).
Create an OptimizationOptions instance.
Enable image compression: options.ImageCompressionOptions.CompressImages = true.
Set desired image quality: options.ImageCompressionOptions.ImageQuality = {int}.
Apply the optimization to the document: {doc}.OptimizeResources(options).
Save the optimized PDF to {output_pdf}.
warnings: Exact fully qualified names for ImageCompressionOptions and OptimizationOptions are inferred from the namespace hierarchy and may differ if the library version changes.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to embed an image into a PDF page by adding it to the page's XImage collection with Flate compression, positioning it via a transformation matrix, and rendering it using PDF operators.
rules: Create a {doc}, add a new page, obtain {page} = {doc}.Pages[<index>]; then add an image stream to {page}.Resources.Images with a specified {filter_type} (e.g., ImageFilterType.Flate) and retrieve the resulting XImage via {ximage} = {page}.Resources.Images[{page}.Resources.Images.Count];
Wrap image drawing commands between GSave and GRestore operators to isolate graphics state changes on {page}.Contents.

Compute a placement {matrix} using a Rectangle {rect} (lower-left and upper-right coordinates) and instantiate a Matrix with the six-element array { [rect.URX-rect.LLX, 0, 0, rect.URY-rect.LLY, rect.LLX, rect.LLY] }; then add a ConcatenateMatrix({matrix}) operator to {page}.Contents before drawing the image.
Render the image by adding a Do({ximage}.Name) operator to {page}.Contents after the matrix operator.
warnings: Aspose.Pdf collections are 1‑based; retrieving the last added image via Count works only because Count returns the 1‑based index of the newly added entry.
The Matrix class resides in Aspose.Pdf.Operators; ensure the correct namespace is imported.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to insert selected pages from one PDF into another PDF at a specific position using the PdfFileEditor.Insert method.
rules: Use PdfFileEditor.Insert({input_pdf}, {int}, {input_pdf}, {int[]}, {output_pdf}) to insert specific pages from a source PDF into a target PDF after the given page number.
Provide the pages to insert as an integer array ({int[]}) representing the 1‑based page numbers in the source document.
warnings: Insert overwrites the output file if it already exists.
Page numbers are 1‑based; ensure the correct indices are supplied.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to insert a consecutive range of pages from one PDF file into another PDF at a specified position using PdfFileEditor.
rules: Instantiate Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor and call Insert({input_pdf}, {int} insertPosition, {input_pdf} sourcePdf, {int} startPage, {int} endPage, {output_pdf}) to insert pages from sourcePdf into input_pdf at the given position.
Page numbers are 1‑based and the range defined by startPage and endPage is inclusive.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to insert a range of pages from one PDF into another PDF using stream objects with the PdfFileEditor.Insert method.
rules: Open the destination PDF ({input_pdf}) and the source PDF ({input_pdf}) as FileStream objects with FileMode.Open, and open an output FileStream with FileMode.Create.

Call PdfFileEditor.Insert({input_stream}, {int startPageDest}, {source_stream}, {int startPageSrc}, {int endPageSrc}, {output_stream}) to insert pages {startPageSrc}-{endPageSrc} from the source PDF into the destination PDF after page {startPageDest}. Page numbers are 1‑based.
Dispose or close all streams after the Insert operation to release file handles.
warnings: The example does not explicitly close or dispose the FileStream objects; callers should ensure streams are properly disposed.
Insert expects the page range to be valid and within the source PDF; no validation is shown.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to insert selected pages from one PDF into another PDF at a specified position using file streams with the PdfFileEditor class.
rules: Use PdfFileEditor.Insert({input_pdf}_stream, {int}_position, {insert_pdf}_stream, {int[]}_pagesToInsert, {output_pdf}_stream) to insert the specified pages from {insert_pdf} into {input_pdf} after page {int}_position, writing the result to {output_pdf}.
Open the source and insert PDFs with FileMode.Open and the destination PDF with FileMode.Create (or appropriate mode) and keep the streams open for the duration of the Insert call.
warnings: The example does not explicitly dispose the FileStream objects; callers should ensure streams are closed/disposed after use.
PdfFileEditor belongs to the Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace, which may require a separate license configuration.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates adding a clickable hyperlink (LinkAnnotation with GoToURIAction) and an associated free‑text label (FreeTextAnnotation) to a PDF page, then saving the updated document.
rules: Create a LinkAnnotation on {page} using a {rect}, optionally set its Border.Width to {int}, assign its Action to a new GoToURIAction({string_literal}) and add it to {page}.Annotations.

Create a FreeTextAnnotation on {page} using a {rect}, set its Contents to {string_literal}, define its DefaultAppearance with a {font} of size {int} and {color}, assign a Border (can reuse the same Border object) and add it to {page}.Annotations.
After modifying annotations, save the {doc} to {output_pdf}.
warnings: The same Border instance is reused for multiple annotations; modifying it later will affect all annotations that share it.
GoToURIAction expects a fully qualified URL (including scheme, e.g., "https://www.aspose.com"); providing only a domain may lead to navigation issues.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add an application (file launch) link to a PDF page using PdfContentEditor, specifying a rectangular region that opens an external file when clicked.
rules: Bind an existing PDF document to a PdfContentEditor instance using {input_pdf} before creating any links.
Create an application link with PdfContentEditor.CreateApplicationLink by providing a {rect}, a target file path {string_literal}, and a valid page number {int} (1‑based).
After modifying the PDF, persist changes with PdfContentEditor.Save to {output_pdf}.
warnings: The example uses System.Drawing.Rectangle which may require the System.Drawing.Common package on non‑Windows platforms.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add an application link (a link that opens an external file) to a PDF page using PdfContentEditor, specifying the link rectangle, target file, page number, and link color, then saving the modified PDF.
rules: Bind {input_pdf} to a PdfContentEditor instance using BindPdf before any editing operations.
Create an application link inside {rect} on page {int} that opens {string_literal} with link color {color} by calling CreateApplicationLink.
Save the modified PDF to {output_pdf} using Save.
warnings: The example relies on System.Drawing types (Rectangle, Color); ensure the appropriate assembly is referenced.
CreateApplicationLink embeds a file launch action which may be disabled by PDF viewers for security reasons.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add a link annotation to a PDF page that launches an external application or file when clicked, using Aspose.PDF's LaunchAction.
rules: Create a LinkAnnotation on {page} with a {rect} defining the clickable area, set its Color to {color} (optional), and assign its Action to a new LaunchAction({doc}, {string_literal}) to launch a file or application.
Add the configured LinkAnnotation to {page}.Annotations before saving {doc} to persist the interactive link.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to attach a JavaScript action that executes when the PDF document is closed by using PdfContentEditor to add a document‑level additional action.
rules: To run JavaScript when a PDF is closed, bind the PDF with PdfContentEditor.BindPdf({input_pdf}) then call PdfContentEditor.AddDocumentAdditionalAction(PdfContentEditor.DocumentClose, {string_literal}) before saving.
Typical workflow: instantiate PdfContentEditor → BindPdf({input_pdf}) → AddDocumentAdditionalAction(...) → Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The exact enum type for DocumentClose is inferred; if the SDK version uses a different enum name, adjust accordingly.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add a clickable area to a PDF that opens another PDF document at a specified page range using PdfContentEditor.
rules: Bind an existing PDF to a PdfContentEditor instance with BindPdf({input_pdf}).
Create a document link using CreatePdfDocumentLink({rect}, {target_pdf}, {int pageStart}, {int pageEnd}) where {rect} defines the clickable region.
Persist the changes by calling Save({output_pdf}) on the same PdfContentEditor instance.
warnings: Method signatures are inferred from usage; exact overloads may differ.
System.Drawing.Rectangle is required for the rectangle parameter, which is not part of Aspose.PDF.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a link annotation on a PDF page that navigates to a specific page of an external PDF document and saves the modified PDF.
rules: To create a document link, instantiate a LinkAnnotation with {page} and a {rect}, set its Color to {color}, assign its Action to a GoToRemoteAction pointing to {string_literal} (target PDF) and {int} (target page).
Add the configured LinkAnnotation to {page}.Annotations before saving {doc} to persist the link.
warnings: The exact namespace for GoToRemoteAction is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf; it may reside in a sub‑namespace such as Aspose.Pdf.Actions.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add a JavaScript action (e.g., an alert) to a PDF page using PdfContentEditor, specifying the clickable rectangle, target page, and link color, then saving the modified document.
rules: Bind a PDF document with PdfContentEditor.BindPdf({input_pdf}) before performing any link or annotation operations.

Create a JavaScript link using PdfContentEditor.CreateJavaScriptLink({string_literal}, {rect}, {int}, {color}) where {string_literal} is the JavaScript code, {rect} defines the clickable area, {int} is the 1‑based page number, and {color} sets the link border color.
Persist the changes by calling PdfContentEditor.Save({output_pdf}) after all modifications are done.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create internal (local) hyperlinks that navigate to specific pages within the same PDF by attaching a LocalHyperlink to a TextFragment, and how to start a TextFragment on a new page using the IsInNewPage flag.
rules: Instantiate a {text_fragment} (Aspose.Pdf.Text.TextFragment) and assign a {link} (Aspose.Pdf.LocalHyperlink) with its TargetPageNumber set to the desired page number, then set text_fragment.Hyperlink = link.
To place a TextFragment on a new page within the same document, set text_fragment.IsInNewPage = true before adding it to the page's Paragraphs collection.
Add the configured TextFragment to a page by calling page.Paragraphs.Add(text_fragment) after all hyperlink and pagination properties are set.
warnings: The target page number must exist in the document at the time of navigation; otherwise an exception may be thrown at runtime.
IsInNewPage only creates a new page when the TextFragment is added to the current page's Paragraphs collection; adding it to a different page object will not have the intended effect.

semantic_summary: Shows how to add a local (internal) link annotation to a PDF page using PdfContentEditor. The example opens an existing PDF, defines a rectangular area, creates a link that jumps to another page, and saves the modified document.
rules: Bind {input_pdf} to a PdfContentEditor instance using BindPdf before any modifications.
Define a {rect} (System.Drawing.Rectangle) that specifies the link area on the source page.
Call CreateLocalLink({rect}, {int pageNumber}, {int destinationPage}, {color}) to create an internal link from the specified page to the destination page.
Save the updated document to {output_pdf} using Save after all link operations are complete.
warnings: The rectangle coordinates are expressed in points; callers must ensure they correspond to the target page layout.
CreateLocalLink expects the source page number (first int) and the destination page number (second int) to be 1‑based.
System.Drawing types are used for geometry and color; ensure the appropriate assembly is referenced.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to extract link annotations from a PDF page by using an AnnotationSelector with a LinkAnnotation and then accessing the selected annotations.
rules: To collect link annotations from a specific {page}, create a LinkAnnotation with that {page} and a placeholder {rect} (e.g., Rectangle.Trivial), wrap it in an AnnotationSelector, and call {page}.Accept(selector).
After invoking Accept, retrieve the extracted annotations via selector.Selected, which yields an IList of Annotation objects; individual links can be accessed by index (e.g., selector.Selected[0]).
warnings: The example only accesses the first annotation in the selected list; iterate over selector.Selected to handle all links.
Saving the document after extraction does not modify the PDF; the Save call is optional if no changes are made.

semantic_summary: The example loads a PDF, iterates through each page, extracts all link annotations, and prints the destination URLs of hyperlink actions.
rules: Load a PDF document with `new Document({input_pdf})` and assign to {doc}.
Iterate over `{doc}.Pages` to obtain each {page}.
Create an `AnnotationSelector` using a `LinkAnnotation` constructed with the current {page} and a rectangle (e.g., `Rectangle.Trivial`).
Call `{page}.Accept(selector)` to populate the selector with matching annotations.
Retrieve the selected annotations via `selector.Selected` which returns an `IList<Annotation>`.
For each `LinkAnnotation` in the list, cast its `Action` property to `GoToURIAction` to access the `URI` string representing the hyperlink destination.
warnings: The cast `(a.Action as GoToURIAction)` assumes the link action is a URI; if the action type differs, the result will be null and accessing `.URI` will throw.

semantic_summary: Shows how to retrieve the target URL and the visible text of hyperlink (LinkAnnotation) objects in a PDF document using Aspose.PDF.
rules: Iterate over {page}.Annotations, filter for instances of LinkAnnotation, and cast the annotation's Action to GoToURIAction to read the hyperlink URI via the URI property.

Create a TextAbsorber, set TextSearchOptions.LimitToPageBounds = true and TextSearchOptions.Rectangle = {rect} (the LinkAnnotation's Rect), then call {page}.Accept(absorber) to capture the text within the hyperlink area as absorber.Text.
warnings: The example assumes the LinkAnnotation's Action is always a GoToURIAction; other action types (e.g., GoToRemoteAction) would require additional handling.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to remove a document‑open action (e.g., JavaScript) from an existing PDF using the PdfContentEditor facade.
rules: Bind the target PDF to a PdfContentEditor instance with {input_pdf} before invoking any editing methods.
Call RemoveDocumentOpenAction() on the bound editor to delete the document‑open action.
Persist the changes by calling Save({output_pdf}) after all modifications.
warnings: The local variable 'rectangle' is declared but not used; it has no effect on the operation.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to delete a PDF document's open action (e.g., JavaScript that runs on opening) by setting the OpenAction property to null and saving the updated file.
rules: Set {doc}.OpenAction = null to remove any action that executes when the PDF is opened.
Persist the changes by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The exact type of the OpenAction property is not captured; it is assumed to be assignable from null.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to modify an existing link annotation in a PDF by assigning a new GoToURIAction that points to a web address and then saving the updated document.
rules: Load a PDF document: {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});
Retrieve a specific page (1‑based index): {page} = {doc}.Pages[{int}];
Access a link annotation by index (1‑based) and cast: {linkAnnot} = (LinkAnnotation){page}.Annotations[{int}];
Replace the annotation's action with a URI action: {linkAnnot}.Action = new GoToURIAction({string_literal});
Save the modified document: {doc}.Save({output_pdf});
warnings: Aspose.PDF uses 1‑based indexing for Pages and Annotations collections; ensure the indices exist to avoid out‑of‑range errors.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to modify an existing link annotation to point to a different PDF file and change its destination coordinates/zoom using a GoToRemoteAction.
rules: Retrieve a specific link annotation: var link = (LinkAnnotation)document.Pages[{int}].Annotations[{int}];
Cast the annotation's Action to GoToRemoteAction: var remote = (GoToRemoteAction)link.Action;
Update the remote link's destination using XYZExplicitDestination: remote.Destination = new XYZExplicitDestination({int page}, {float left}, {float top}, {float zoom});
Change the target file of the remote link: remote.File = new FileSpecification({string_literal path to external PDF});
Save the modified document: document.Save({string_literal output path});
warnings: Aspose.Pdf uses 1‑based indexing for Pages collection; ensure correct page number.
Annotations collection indexing may also be 1‑based; verify existence before casting.
Casting to LinkAnnotation and GoToRemoteAction will throw if the annotation type differs.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to set a PDF's open action so that the document opens on a specific page with a defined zoom level using a GoToAction and XYZExplicitDestination.
rules: Load {input_pdf} into a {doc}, retrieve the target {page} (1‑based index), create a {GoToAction} for that page, assign an {XYZExplicitDestination}( {page}, {float:left}, {float:top}, {float:zoom} ) to the action's Destination, set {doc}.OpenAction = action, then save to {output_pdf}.

When constructing an XYZExplicitDestination for a page view, use X = 0, Y = page.Rect.Height to start at the top‑left corner, and provide a zoom factor (e.g., 1.0) to control the initial magnification.
warnings: The example assumes the Aspose.Pdf.Annotations namespace is imported for GoToAction; otherwise the fully qualified name must be used.
Page indices in Aspose.Pdf are 1‑based; attempting to access a non‑existent page will throw an exception.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to locate the text underneath a LinkAnnotation on a PDF page, expand the search area, and change the text color using Aspose.PDF.
rules: Iterate over {doc}.Pages[{int}].Annotations and filter for annotations of type LinkAnnotation.

Create a {rect} by expanding the LinkAnnotation.Rect (e.g., +/-10 units) and initialize a TextFragmentAbsorber with TextSearchOptions({rect}) to capture {text_fragment}s within that area on the same {page}.
For each captured {text_fragment}, set {text_fragment}.TextState.ForegroundColor to the desired {color} (e.g., Color.Red) to modify its appearance.
After processing, call {doc}.Save({output_pdf}) to write the changes to a new PDF file.
warnings: The example only processes annotations on the first page (index 1).
Rectangle expansion offsets are hard‑coded; adjust as needed for different PDFs.
Assumes that the text to be recolored lies entirely within the expanded rectangle.

semantic_summary: Shows how to modify the destination of an existing link annotation in a PDF by accessing its GoToAction and assigning a new XYZExplicitDestination, then saving the updated document.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} using new Document({input_pdf}).
Access a specific page {page} via {doc}.Pages[{int}].
Retrieve an annotation from the page's Annotations collection by index {int} and cast to the expected type, e.g., {link_annotation} = (LinkAnnotation){page}.Annotations[{int}].
Obtain the action of a link annotation and cast to GoToAction: {go_to_action} = (GoToAction){link_annotation}.Action.

Create a new XYZExplicitDestination with parameters (pageNumber, left, top, zoom) and assign it to the action's Destination: {go_to_action}.Destination = new XYZExplicitDestination({int}, {float}, {float}, {float}).
Save the modified document to {output_pdf} via {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: Aspose.Pdf uses 1‑based indexing for Pages and Annotations collections; ensure correct indices.
Casting annotations without checking their actual type may cause InvalidCastException if the annotation at the specified index is not a LinkAnnotation.

semantic_summary: Shows how to generate a booklet PDF by specifying custom left‑hand and right‑hand page sequences using the PdfFileEditor.MakeBooklet method.
rules: Instantiate {class:PdfFileEditor} and invoke MakeBooklet({input_pdf}, {output_pdf}, {int[]} leftPages, {int[]} rightPages) to reorder pages into a booklet layout.
Provide {int[]} leftPages and {int[]} rightPages containing 1‑based page numbers from the source document; these arrays define the order of pages on the left and right sides of each sheet.
warnings: MakeBooklet belongs to the Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace, which may be deprecated in future releases; verify compatibility with the target Aspose.PDF version.
Page numbers are 1‑based; supplying out‑of‑range numbers will cause a runtime error.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a booklet PDF by specifying custom left‑ and right‑page order using Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor with input and output streams.
rules: Use PdfFileEditor.MakeBooklet({input_stream}, {output_stream}, {int[]} leftPages, {int[]} rightPages) to reorder pages into a booklet where leftPages and rightPages define the sequence of left‑hand and right‑hand pages.
When working with streams, open the source PDF with a read‑only FileStream and create the destination PDF with a writeable FileStream before invoking MakeBooklet.
warnings: MakeBooklet expects the leftPages and rightPages arrays to correspond correctly; mismatched lengths may cause unexpected ordering.
Streams are not automatically closed; callers should dispose or close them after the operation.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a booklet version of an existing PDF by specifying the desired output page size using the PdfFileEditor facade.
rules: Use PdfFileEditor.MakeBooklet({input_pdf}, {output_pdf}, {page_size}) to convert a PDF into booklet layout with the given page size.
The {page_size} argument must be a value from the PageSize enum (e.g., PageSize.A5) that defines the target dimensions of the booklet pages.
warnings: MakeBooklet may require the source PDF to have a page count compatible with booklet pagination (typically a multiple of 4); the example does not validate this.

semantic_summary: Shows how to generate a booklet PDF from an existing document by using streams for input and output and specifying the target page size.

Create FileStream objects for the source and destination PDFs, opening the source with FileMode.Open and the destination with FileMode.Create.
warnings: The example uses the Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace, which may be deprecated in newer Aspose.PDF versions; consider using the main Aspose.Pdf API if available.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a booklet PDF from an existing document by specifying the target page size and custom left/right page ordering using PdfFileEditor.
rules: Instantiate Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor and call MakeBooklet({input_pdf}, {output_pdf}, {page_size}, {int[] leftPages}, {int[] rightPages}) to generate a booklet.

Provide two integer arrays (leftPages and rightPages) containing 1‑based page numbers from the source PDF; these arrays define the ordering of pages on the left and right sides of each booklet spread.
Select a target page size using the Aspose.Pdf.PageSize enum (e.g., PageSize.A5) to control the dimensions of the resulting booklet pages.
warnings: The leftPages and rightPages arrays must correctly reference existing pages; mismatched lengths or invalid page numbers will cause runtime errors.
MakeBooklet overwrites the output file if it already exists.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to generate a booklet PDF from an existing document using streams, specifying a target page size and custom left/right page ordering with Aspose.Pdf.Facades.
rules: Instantiate {type=Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor} and call {method=MakeBooklet} with an input {type=System.IO.Stream} ({input_pdf}), an output {type=System.IO.Stream} ({output_pdf}), a {type=Aspose.Pdf.PageSize} ({page_size}), an int[] of left page numbers ({leftPages}), and an int[] of right page numbers ({rightPages}).

When using {method=MakeBooklet}, the {leftPages} and {rightPages} arrays define the sequence of source pages that will appear on the left and right sides of each booklet sheet; each element must be a valid 1‑based page index present in {input_pdf}.
warnings: The example uses FileStream objects; ensure streams are properly disposed in production code.
MakeBooklet overloads may also accept file paths; this rule is specific to the stream‑based overload.

semantic_summary: Shows how to generate a booklet version of an existing PDF by calling PdfFileEditor.MakeBooklet with input and output file paths.
rules: Instantiate Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor and invoke MakeBooklet({input_pdf}, {output_pdf}) to create a booklet from a source PDF.
Provide absolute or relative file system paths for both the source and destination PDFs; the method does not require loading the document into a Document object.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to generate a booklet PDF from an existing document by using input and output streams with the Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor class.
rules: Instantiate Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor and call MakeBooklet({input_pdf_stream}, {output_pdf_stream}) to convert a PDF into booklet layout.
Open the source PDF stream with FileMode.Open (read) and the destination stream with FileMode.Create (write) before invoking MakeBooklet.

Provide the source PDF as a readable stream (e.g., FileStream opened with FileMode.Open) and the destination as a writable stream (e.g., FileStream opened with FileMode.Create).

Retrieve the rotation of a specific page using GetPageRotation({int pageNumber}), which returns the rotation angle in degrees.
Obtain the size of a predefined page box via GetPageBoxSize({int pageNumber}, {string_literal boxName}), where boxName may be "trim", "art", "bleed", "crop" or "media".

Box name strings are case‑sensitive and must match the supported names exactly.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to resize the content area of selected PDF pages by specifying margin values (as percentages) using the PdfFileEditor facade. The example loads a document, defines left/right/top/bottom margins, applies the resizing to pages 1 and 2, and saves the result.

When the new width or height should be derived automatically from the margins, pass null for the corresponding argument in the ContentsResizeParameters constructor.
warnings: The example uses the Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace, which may be deprecated in newer library versions; ensure the facade API is still available.
Null values in ContentsResizeParameters rely on overload behavior; verify the constructor signature matches the library version.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to use PdfPageEditor to modify page layout properties such as origin offset and zoom factor, and shows the pattern for applying page rotations before saving the updated PDF.

Shift the page content origin with MovePosition({int}, {int}) to move horizontally and vertically from the default (0,0) coordinate.
Set the overall page zoom by assigning a {float} value to the Zoom property (1.0 = 100% zoom).
Optionally define page-specific rotations in a Hashtable mapping page numbers ({int}) to rotation angles in degrees ({int}) and assign it to PdfPageEditor.PageRotations before saving.
warnings: The example comments out the actual assignment to PageRotations, so the rotation rule is inferred but not demonstrated.

semantic_summary: Shows how to load a PDF, locate a line annotation on a page, and read its measurement information such as scale ratio and area format details.

Access a specific page (1‑based) from {doc}: var {page} = {doc}.Pages[{int}];
Retrieve a line annotation from {page} by index (1‑based) and cast it: var {annotation} = {page}.Annotations[{int}] as LineAnnotation;

Read measurement properties from the line annotation: {annotation}.Measure.ScaleRatio, {annotation}.Measure.AreaFormat[{int}].UnitLabel, {annotation}.Measure.AreaFormat[{int}].ConvresionFactor, {annotation}.Measure.AreaFormat[{int}].FractionSeparator.
warnings: Aspose.Pdf uses 1‑based indexing for Pages, Annotations, and AreaFormat collections; ensure correct indices.
The property name 'ConvresionFactor' appears to be a typo in the sample; the actual API property is likely 'ConversionFactor'.

Demonstrates how to add a line annotation with measurement information to a PDF page, customize its visual style, configure measurement formatting, and display a caption.
rules: Create a {doc} from an input PDF file, obtain the target {page} (e.g., {doc}.Pages[1]), and define a {rect} that bounds the annotation.
Instantiate a LineAnnotation with the signature LineAnnotation({page}, {rect}, {point_start}, {point_end}) and add it to {page}.Annotations.

Set visual properties on the line annotation: {color} via .Color, leader line offset via .LeaderLine, extension length via .LeaderLineExtension, and arrow styles via .StartingStyle and .EndingStyle using the LineEnding enum.

Create a Measure object linked to the line (line.Measure = new Measure(line)) and configure its distance format by creating a NumberFormatList, adding a NumberFormat entry, and setting properties such as UnitLabel, FractionSeparator, and ConvresionFactor.
Assign caption text to the line via .Contents, enable its display with .ShowCaption = true, and position it using .CaptionPosition (e.g., CaptionPosition.Top).
Save the modified document to an output PDF file using {doc}.Save({string_literal}).
warnings: The exact namespace for Color may be Aspose.Pdf.Color or System.Drawing.Color depending on the project references.
Point and Rectangle types are assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.Point and Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle; alternative overloads may exist.

Set visual properties on the PolylineAnnotation such as {color}, StartingStyle, and EndingStyle to define line color and arrowheads.

Enable measurement on the annotation by assigning a new Measure({annotation}) to its Measure property, then configure distance formatting via Measure.DistanceFormat (a NumberFormatList) and set UnitLabel on the desired NumberFormat entry.
warnings: The Point and Rectangle types used are from Aspose.Pdf namespace, not System.Drawing; ensure correct using directives.
Measure.NumberFormatList indexing starts at 1 in this example, which may differ from typical zero‑based collections.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create an XForm (form XObject) containing an image, add it to a page's resources, and draw the XForm multiple times on the page using GSave/GRestore and ConcatenateMatrix operators to control positioning.
rules: Create an XForm on {page} of {doc} using XForm.CreateNewForm({page}, {doc}), add it to {page}.Resources.Forms, then within the XForm's Contents add a GSave, a ConcatenateMatrix({float_width},0,0,{float_height},0,0) to set size, load an image stream from {string_literal} and add it to form.Resources.Images, retrieve the XImage via form.Resources.Images[form.Resources.Images.Count], draw the image with a Do operator using the XImage.Name, and finish with a GRestore.

To draw an existing XForm {form} at a specific location on a page, insert a GSave, apply a translation matrix with ConcatenateMatrix(1,0,0,1,{float_x},{float_y}), invoke a Do operator with {form}.Name, and close with a GRestore.

When inserting custom operators into an existing page's content stream, prepend a GSave operator at the start of the OperatorCollection and append a GRestore at the end to preserve the original graphics state and avoid unintended transformations.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to embed an image onto a PDF page by adding the image to page resources and using low‑level graphics operators (GSave, ConcatenateMatrix, Do, GRestore) to control placement.
rules: To place an image on {page}, first add the image stream ({image_stream}) to {page}.Resources.Images, then append the operators in order: GSave, ConcatenateMatrix (with a matrix derived from the desired {rect}), Do (using the image name retrieved from the resources), and finally GRestore.

Create a placement matrix from a {rect} using: new Matrix(new double[] { rect.URX - rect.LLX, 0, 0, rect.URY - rect.LLY, rect.LLX, rect.LLY }). This matrix maps the image to the rectangle's coordinates.
After modifying the page contents, persist the changes by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The example accesses the newly added image via page.Resources.Images[page.Resources.Images.Count]; this relies on 1‑based indexing of the Images collection and may be error‑prone if the collection indexing changes.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to rotate each page of a PDF by swapping its MediaBox dimensions, adjusting the origin, and setting the page rotation flag.
rules: Iterate over each {page} in {doc}.Pages and compute new dimensions: newHeight = {page}.MediaBox.Width, newWidth = {page}.MediaBox.Height; adjust lower‑left Y: newLLY = {page}.MediaBox.LLY + ({page}.MediaBox.Height - newHeight); then assign {page}.MediaBox = new Rectangle({page}.MediaBox.LLX, newLLY, {page}.MediaBox.LLX + newWidth, newLLY + newHeight).
After updating the MediaBox (and optionally the CropBox), set the rotation metadata: {page}.Rotate = Rotation.{rotationEnum} (e.g., on90) to indicate the page should be displayed rotated.
If the original PDF defines a CropBox, mirror the MediaBox changes by assigning the same new Rectangle to {page}.CropBox to keep cropping consistent.
warnings: Helper methods HasOnlyWhiteColor and IsBlankPage are defined but not used in the main flow.
The example only shows a 90‑degree rotation; other Rotation enum values require analogous box adjustments.
Assumes the PDF coordinate system origin is at the lower‑left corner; different origins may need different offset calculations.

semantic_summary: Shows how to merge two PDF documents by appending all pages of a second document to the first one and saving the combined result.

Append pages from another PDF: {doc}.Pages.Add({other_doc}.Pages);
Persist the merged document: {doc}.Save({output_pdf});
warnings: The Add method copies all pages; selective page ranges require additional filtering.
If {output_pdf} already exists it will be overwritten without warning.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to open an existing PDF, delete a specific page by its 1‑based index, and save the modified document.

Persist the changes by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The Delete method expects a 1‑based page index and will throw if the index is out of range.

semantic_summary: Shows how to inspect each page of a PDF to determine its color space (black‑and‑white, grayscale, RGB or undefined) using Aspose.Pdf.
rules: Load a PDF file from {input_pdf} into a {doc} by invoking `new Document(path)`.
Iterate over pages using a 1‑based index {int} from 1 to `{doc}.Pages.Count` and obtain the page's color type via `{doc}.Pages[{int}].ColorType`, which yields a {color} (ColorType) enum.
Use a switch (or equivalent) on the retrieved {color} to handle the defined values: BlackAndWhite, Grayscale, Rgb, and Undefined.
warnings: Page indexing in Aspose.Pdf is 1‑based; accessing index 0 will throw an exception.
ColorType may return Undefined for PDFs that do not explicitly specify a color space.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to retrieve each page's MediaBox rectangle from a PDF document and compute new dimensions for re‑orienting the page to landscape while preserving height.

Iterate over each {page} in {doc}.Pages collection.
Access the page size via {rect} = {page}.MediaBox and use {rect}.Width and {rect}.Height for dimension calculations.
warnings: The example only calculates new dimensions; it does not apply them back to the page.
Assumes the original orientation is portrait; the calculation may not be appropriate for all page sizes.

semantic_summary: Shows how to obtain a page's width and height before and after rotating the page by using GetPageRect and the Rotate property.
rules: To ensure a page object is available, use: {doc}.Pages.Count > 0 ? {doc}.Pages[1] : {doc}.Pages.Add();
Retrieve the current page dimensions (taking rotation into account) with: var rect = {page}.GetPageRect(true); // rect.Width, rect.Height
Rotate a page by assigning a Rotation enum value: {page}.Rotate = Rotation.on90; // other values: on180, on270, on0
warnings: Aspose.PDF page indices are 1‑based; accessing Pages[1] returns the first page.
GetPageRect(true) returns dimensions after applying the page's rotation; using false would give the original media box size.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to print a PDF document using Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfViewer with custom printer settings, check the print job status for errors, set a custom printer job name, and optionally run the print operation under an impersonated user context.

Configure PrinterSettings with required properties (PrinterName, PrintFileName, PrintToFile, FromPage, ToPage, PrintRange) before printing.
Create a PageSettings object, set its PaperSize (e.g., new PaperSize("A4", 827, 1169)) and Margins, then assign the PaperSize to printerSettings.DefaultPageSettings.
Print the document using PdfViewer.PrintDocumentWithSettings(pageSettings, printerSettings).
After printing, inspect PdfViewer.PrintStatus: if null the job succeeded; if it is an Exception, handle the error.
Optionally set a custom printer job name via PdfViewer.PrinterJobName = {string_literal};
When printing under a different user, wrap the print call inside an impersonation context (e.g., using new Impersonator(...)) to execute with the desired credentials.
warnings: The Impersonator class is not part of Aspose.PDF; it must be provided by the user environment.
PrinterName must correspond to an installed printer on the host machine; otherwise printing will fail.
PrintToFile works only with printers that support file output (e.g., XPS Document Writer).

Shows how to convert a PDF document to a PostScript file by binding the PDF to a PdfViewer and printing it with custom PrinterSettings that target a PS printer and output to a file.
rules: Bind the source PDF {input_pdf} to a PdfViewer instance using BindPdf before any printing operation.

Assign the configured PrinterSettings to PdfViewer.PrintDocumentWithSettings to perform the print to file.
Set PdfViewer.PrintPageDialog = false to suppress the interactive print dialog when printing programmatically.
Call PdfViewer.Close after printing to release underlying resources.

Output file is a PostScript (.ps) file, not a PDF.

semantic_summary: Shows how to print a PDF with custom printer and page settings and how to adjust per‑page settings (e.g., paper source) via the PdfViewer facade.
rules: To print a PDF with specific printer and page settings: create a PdfViewer, call BindPdf({doc}), configure System.Drawing.Printing.PrinterSettings (e.g., PrinterName, DefaultPageSettings.PaperSize), configure System.Drawing.Printing.PageSettings (Margins, PaperSize), optionally subscribe to PdfViewer.PdfQueryPageSettings, then invoke PrintDocumentWithSettings(pageSettings, printerSettings).

For per‑page customization, handle the PdfViewer.PdfQueryPageSettings event; inside the handler use the provided PdfPrintPageInfo to inspect {page}.PageNumber and set queryPageSettingsEventArgs.PageSettings properties (e.g., PaperSource) accordingly.
If document modifications are required before printing, bind the same or another PDF with PdfPageEditor before invoking the print operation.
warnings: The example code is commented out; actual execution requires uncommenting the relevant lines and ensuring the event subscription (PdfViewer.PdfQueryPageSettings) is active.
PdfPrintPageInfo type location is inferred from the example; verify the exact namespace in the Aspose.PDF version being used.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to print selected page ranges of a PDF to XPS files using PdfViewer with custom printer and page settings, and how to chain multiple print jobs via the EndPrint event.
rules: To print a specific page range of {doc} to a file, bind the PDF with PdfViewer, set PrinterSettings.PrintToFile = true, assign PrinterSettings.PrintFileName = {string_literal}, configure PrinterSettings.FromPage = {int}, PrinterSettings.ToPage = {int}, PrinterSettings.Duplex = {enum}, and call viewer.PrintDocumentWithSettings(pageSettings, printerSettings).

To execute multiple sequential print jobs, store each job's parameters (FromPage, ToPage, OutputFile, Duplex) in a collection, subscribe to viewer.EndPrint, and in the handler update the same PrinterSettings with the next job's values before invoking viewer.PrintDocumentWithSettings again.

To define a custom paper size and zero margins, create a new Aspose.Pdf.Printing.PaperSize("A4", width, height), assign it to PageSettings.PaperSize, and set PageSettings.Margins = new Aspose.Pdf.Devices.Margins(0,0,0,0).
warnings: PrinterSettings and PageSettings are from System.Drawing.Printing, not Aspose.PDF; the example assumes the "Microsoft XPS Document Writer" printer is installed on the system.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load a PDF with PdfViewer, configure printing options, and send the document to the default printer (optionally via a PrintDialog) using Aspose.Pdf printing classes.
rules: Bind the PDF file {input_pdf} to a PdfViewer instance before any printing operation.
Set PdfViewer.AutoResize and PdfViewer.AutoRotate to {bool} values to let the viewer adjust size and orientation automatically.
Create Aspose.Pdf.Printing.PrinterSettings and Aspose.Pdf.Printing.PageSettings objects; assign the target printer name via System.Drawing.Printing.PrintDocument.PrinterSettings.PrinterName.
Optionally configure PageSettings.PaperSize with a new PaperSize(string name, {int} width, {int} height) and PageSettings.Margins with a Margins({int} left, {int} top, {int} right, {int} bottom).
Invoke PdfViewer.PrintDocumentWithSettings(pageSettings, printerSettings) to send the document to the printer.
When a user confirmation is required, display a System.Windows.Forms.PrintDialog and call PrintDocumentWithSettings only if the dialog result is OK.
warnings: Requires a physical or virtual printer installed on the host machine.
Uses System.Drawing.Printing and System.Windows.Forms which are Windows‑specific; may not work on non‑Windows platforms without additional configuration.

rules: To print a PDF to a specific printer, instantiate PdfViewer, call BindPdf({input_pdf}), set viewer properties (AutoResize, AutoRotate, PrintPageDialog), create System.Drawing.Printing.PrinterSettings and PageSettings, assign PrinterName (e.g., "Microsoft XPS Document Writer"), optionally set PaperSize and Margins, then invoke PrintDocumentWithSettings(PageSettings, PrinterSettings).
Suppress the native print page dialog by setting PdfViewer.PrintPageDialog = false before calling PrintDocumentWithSettings.

To generate an output file (e.g., XPS) instead of sending to a physical printer, set PrinterSettings.PrintToFile = true and PrinterSettings.PrintFileName = {output_file}, then call PrintDocumentWithSettings(PrinterSettings) (overload without PageSettings).
Enable native system font rendering (embedding fonts in the printed output) by setting PdfViewer.RenderingOptions.SystemFontsNativeRendering = true prior to printing.
warnings: The example relies on System.Drawing.Printing which is Windows‑specific; it may not work on non‑Windows platforms.
PrintDocumentWithSettings has multiple overloads; ensure the correct overload (PageSettings, PrinterSettings) or (PrinterSettings) is used as shown.
The printer name must correspond to an installed printer driver on the host machine; otherwise printing will fail.
