rules: Create a PdfFileStamp instance and call BindPdf({input_pdf}) before any stamping operations.
Instantiate a Stamp, then call BindImage({string_literal}) to set the image source for the stamp.
Use SetOrigin({int}, {int}) on the Stamp to define the X and Y coordinates of the stamp's lower‑left corner.
Assign Rotation = {float} on the Stamp to rotate the image by the specified angle (degrees).
Set IsBackground = {bool} on the Stamp to control whether the stamp appears behind (true) or in front of page content.
Call AddStamp(stamp) on the PdfFileStamp to apply the configured stamp to all pages of the bound document.
Save the modified document with Save({output_pdf}) and then close the PdfFileStamp with Close().
warnings: The Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace is considered legacy; newer versions recommend using Aspose.Pdf.Document and related classes.
Coordinate units are points; callers must convert from other units if needed.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add an image stamp to a specific page of an existing PDF using the PdfFileStamp and Stamp classes from Aspose.PDF for .NET.
rules: Bind an existing PDF to a PdfFileStamp instance with BindPdf({input_pdf}) before any stamping operations.

Create a Stamp, bind an image via BindImage({image_path}), set its position using SetOrigin({float}, {float}), optionally rotate it with Rotation = {float}, set IsBackground = {bool}, and limit its application to specific pages using Pages = new int[] { {int}, ... }. Then add the stamp to the PDF with PdfFileStamp.AddStamp(stamp).
After all stamps are added, persist the changes by calling PdfFileStamp.Save({output_pdf}) and release resources with PdfFileStamp.Close().
warnings: Method signatures (e.g., SetOrigin) may accept double rather than float; adjust types accordingly.
The example uses hard‑coded page numbers; ensure the page index matches the target document.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add sequential page numbers to a PDF using PdfFileStamp, including custom formatting and numbering styles.
rules: Create a {doc} of type PdfFileStamp, call BindPdf({input_pdf}) to load the source PDF, optionally set StartingNumber = {int}, then invoke AddPageNumber(FormattedText({text_fragment}, {color}, {color}, {font}, {enum:EncodingType}, {bool}, {int}), {int}) to stamp all pages, finally Save({output_pdf}) and Close().

To use a predefined numbering style, set PdfFileStamp.NumberingStyle = {enum:NumberingStyle} before calling AddPageNumber("#"), where "#" is the placeholder for the current page number; then Save({output_pdf}) and Close().
warnings: The overload AddPageNumber(formattedText, 0) uses a page index of 0 which in Aspose.PDF means "apply to all pages"; this behavior should be verified for future SDK versions.
EncodingType.Winansi requires the System.Text namespace; ensure appropriate using directives are present.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to apply a PDF page as a stamp to every page of another PDF using PdfFileStamp and Stamp classes, with control over position, rotation, and background rendering.
rules: Bind the target PDF to a PdfFileStamp via BindPdf({input_pdf}) before adding any stamps.
Create a Stamp, bind a source PDF page via BindPdf({stamp_pdf}, {int}) to use as the stamp content.
Configure stamp placement with SetOrigin({int}, {int}) and rotation with Rotation = {float}.
Set IsBackground = {bool} to render the stamp behind existing page content.
Add the configured Stamp to the PdfFileStamp with AddStamp(stamp) to apply it to all pages of the bound document.
warnings: AddStamp without specifying a page range applies the stamp to every page of the bound PDF.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to overlay a specific page from one PDF onto selected pages of another PDF using the PdfFileStamp and Stamp classes, with control over position, rotation, and background placement.
rules: Bind the target document to a PdfFileStamp via PdfFileStamp.BindPdf({input_pdf}); then bind the source PDF page to a Stamp via Stamp.BindPdf({stamp_pdf}, {int});

Configure the visual placement of the stamp: call Stamp.SetOrigin({int}, {int}) for X/Y coordinates, set Stamp.Rotation = {float} (degrees), and optionally set Stamp.IsBackground = {bool} to render behind existing content;

Select the pages of the target document that should receive the stamp by assigning Stamp.Pages = new int[] { {int}, ... }; finally add the stamp with PdfFileStamp.AddStamp(stamp), save the result with PdfFileStamp.Save({output_pdf}), and close the stamp object.
warnings: The example assumes the Aspose.PDF license is set elsewhere; without a valid license, evaluation watermarks may appear.
Coordinate system for SetOrigin uses points (1/72 inch) with origin at the lower‑left corner of the page.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add a PDF page stamp (as a background image) to a specific page of an existing PDF document, configure its position and rotation, and save the modified document.
rules: Load a PDF file into a {doc} instance using new Document({input_pdf}).
Select the target {page} from {doc}.Pages using a 1‑based index.
Instantiate a PdfPageStamp with the selected {page}: new PdfPageStamp({page}).
Set stamp properties such as Background = {bool}, XIndent = {float}, YIndent = {float}, Rotate = {enum_value}.
Add the configured stamp to the same {page} via {page}.AddStamp({stamp}).
Save the modified {doc} to {output_pdf} using {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: Aspose.Pdf uses 1‑based indexing for pages; ensure the correct page number is supplied.
The Rotation enum values (e.g., on180) are case‑sensitive and must match the enum definition.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load a PDF, create a configurable text stamp, place it on a specific page, and save the modified document.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} using new Document({input_pdf}) before applying any stamps.

Create a TextStamp with {string_literal}, configure its properties (Background {bool}, XIndent {int}, YIndent {int}, Rotate {Rotation}, TextState.Font = FontRepository.FindFont({font}), TextState.FontSize = {float}, TextState.FontStyle = {FontStyles}, TextState.ForegroundColor = Color.FromRgb({color})), then add it to a page via {doc}.Pages[{int}].AddStamp(textStamp).
After stamping, persist changes by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: Assigning TextState.FontStyle multiple times overwrites the previous value; combine styles with bitwise OR if needed.
Page indices in Aspose.Pdf are 1‑based; using 0 will throw an exception.
The example uses System.Drawing.Color to obtain an RGB value, which may require adding a reference to System.Drawing.Common in .NET Core projects.

semantic_summary: Shows how to add a text stamp to every page of an existing PDF using PdfFileStamp and Stamp, configuring text formatting, position, rotation and background rendering.
rules: Create a {doc} of type PdfFileStamp and bind it to {input_pdf} via BindPdf.
Instantiate a Stamp, then bind a text fragment using BindLogo(new FormattedText({string_literal}, {color}, {color}, {font}, {encoding}, {bool}, {int})).
Set the stamp's position with SetOrigin({int}, {int}) and optional rotation with Rotation = {float}.
Mark the stamp as background with IsBackground = {bool} if it should appear behind page content.
Add the configured stamp to the PdfFileStamp with AddStamp(stamp) – the stamp is applied to all pages by default.
Save the modified document to {output_pdf} and close the PdfFileStamp.
warnings: The example uses System.Drawing.Color which may require additional platform-specific dependencies.
BindLogo is used for text stamping; this may be confusing because the method name suggests an image logo.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add a rotated, background text stamp to a specific page of an existing PDF using the Aspose.Pdf.Facades API.
rules: Bind the source PDF to a PdfFileStamp instance with PdfFileStamp.BindPdf({input_pdf}).
Create a FormattedText object for the stamp content: new FormattedText({text_fragment}, {color}, {color}, {font}, {EncodingType}, {bool}, {int}) and attach it via Stamp.BindLogo(formattedText).
Configure stamp placement and appearance: Stamp.SetOrigin({int}, {int}); Stamp.Rotation = {float}; Stamp.IsBackground = {bool}.
Limit the stamp to specific pages by setting Stamp.Pages = new int[] { {int}, ... }.
Add the configured stamp to the document with PdfFileStamp.AddStamp(stamp), then save and close using PdfFileStamp.Save({output_pdf}) and PdfFileStamp.Close().
warnings: The Facades API (PdfFileStamp, Stamp) is considered legacy; newer Document API may be preferred.
System.Drawing.Color is used for color parameters, which may not be cross‑platform in .NET 6+ without additional packages.

semantic_summary: Shows how to add distinct text stamps as headers on individual pages of a PDF, customizing alignment, font style, colors, zoom, rotation and background per stamp.
rules: Create a {text_fragment} = new Aspose.Pdf.TextStamp({string_literal}); set {text_fragment}.VerticalAlignment and {text_fragment}.HorizontalAlignment to position the stamp on the page, configure {text_fragment}.TextState (Font, FontSize, FontStyle, ForegroundColor, BackgroundColor) as needed, then add it to a specific page with {doc}.Pages[{int}].AddStamp({text_fragment});
Optional visual adjustments: set {text_fragment}.Zoom = {float} to scale the stamp and {text_fragment}.RotateAngle = {float} to rotate it before adding to the page.
warnings: Page indices are 1‑based; ensure the target page exists in the document.
The Color and Font classes require appropriate using directives (Aspose.Pdf).
FontRepository.FindFont may fail if the specified font is not installed on the host machine.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to reposition an existing stamp on a PDF page using PdfContentEditor by specifying new X and Y coordinates, and then saving the modified document.
api_surface: Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfContentEditor.MoveStamp(int pageId, int stampIndexOrId, double x, double y)
rules: Bind the source PDF to a PdfContentEditor instance with BindPdf({input_pdf}).
Reposition a stamp on a specific page using MoveStamp({page}, {int}, {float}, {float}) where the integer can be either the stamp's index or its unique ID.
Persist the changes by calling Save({output_pdf}) on the same PdfContentEditor instance.
warnings: The example assumes that a stamp with the given index/ID already exists on the specified page; no validation is performed.
MoveStamp uses the same signature for both index and ID, so the caller must know which identifier is appropriate.

semantic_summary: Shows how to enumerate the artifacts on a PDF page and count those whose subtype is a watermark using Aspose.PDF.
rules: To count artifacts of a specific subtype on a page, iterate over {doc}.Pages[{int}].Artifacts and increment a counter when artifact.Subtype == Artifact.ArtifactSubtype.{enum_value}.
Use the Artifact.ArtifactSubtype enum (e.g., Watermark) to filter artifacts by their purpose such as watermarks, stamps, etc.
warnings: Aspose.PDF page collection is 1‑based; the example accesses page 1 explicitly.
Only the first page is examined; extend the loop over {doc}.Pages if needed.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a multi‑line text stamp, set its horizontal, vertical, and text alignments to center, adjust its top margin, and apply it to a specific page of a PDF document.
rules: Create a {text_fragment} using FormattedText, add lines with AddNewLineText({string_literal}), instantiate a TextStamp with the FormattedText, then set stamp.HorizontalAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center, stamp.VerticalAlignment = VerticalAlignment.Center, and stamp.TextAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center to achieve full center alignment on the page.
Add the configured TextStamp to a target page by retrieving the page via {doc}.Pages[{int}] (1‑based index) and invoking AddStamp({stamp}) on that page.
warnings: Page indexing in Aspose.Pdf is 1‑based; ensure the correct page number is used.
TopMargin is specified in points; adjust as needed for layout.

semantic_summary: Shows how to extract an image that was added as an image stamp on a PDF page by using PdfContentEditor to retrieve the stamp information and saving the embedded image to a file.
rules: Bind a PDF document to a PdfContentEditor instance with BindPdf({input_pdf}).
Retrieve all stamps on a specific page using GetStamps({page}) which returns an array of StampInfo objects.
For an image stamp, access the Image property of the corresponding StampInfo to obtain a System.Drawing.Image object.
Persist the extracted image by calling Image.Save({output_image_path}).
warnings: GetStamps returns stamps of any type; the Image property is only valid for image stamps; callers should verify the stamp type before accessing Image.
Saving the image requires System.Drawing.Common (or appropriate platform support) which may not be available on all .NET runtimes.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to extract the textual content embedded in the normal appearance of a stamp annotation within a PDF document.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} and retrieve a {annotation_type} from {page} using index {int}, e.g., {doc}.Pages[{int}].Annotations[{int}] as {annotation_type}.

Obtain the normal appearance XForm of the annotation via {annotation}.Appearance[{string_literal}] and extract its text by creating a TextAbsorber {text_absorber}, calling {text_absorber}.Visit({xform}), then reading {text_absorber}.Text.
warnings: The example assumes the annotation at the specified index is a StampAnnotation and that its appearance dictionary contains a "N" (normal) state.
Hard‑coded indices (page 1, annotation 3) may not be valid for other PDFs.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to add an outline‑only (stroke) text watermark to an existing PDF by configuring a TextState with StrokingColor and StrokeText rendering mode, binding it to a Stamp, and applying visual transformations before saving.
rules: Create a {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document) from {input_pdf}, then instantiate PdfFileStamp with that document.
Instantiate TextState, set StrokingColor to {color}, set RenderingMode to TextRenderingMode.StrokeText, and bind it to a Stamp via Stamp.BindTextState.
Create FormattedText with {string_literal}, fill {color}, font name {font}, encoding {EncodingType}, bold flag {bool}, and size {int}; bind it to the Stamp using Stamp.BindLogo.
Configure Stamp visual properties: SetOrigin(x, y), Opacity = {float}, BlendingSpace = {BlendingColorSpace}, Rotation = {float}, IsBackground = {bool}.
Add the configured Stamp to the PdfFileStamp with AddStamp, then Save to {output_pdf} and close the stamp object.
warnings: Fully qualified names for EncodingType, BlendingColorSpace, and FormattedText are assumed to be in Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace.
Opacity value of 5 in the sample exceeds the typical 0‑1 range; actual acceptable range may depend on library version.

semantic_summary: Shows how to open a PDF document and enumerate the artifacts on a page to obtain information about watermarks such as subtype, text content, and bounding rectangle.
rules: Load a PDF by creating a {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});
Iterate over {doc}.Pages[{int}].Artifacts to access each {artifact} and read its Subtype, Text, and Rectangle properties for watermark details.
warnings: Artifacts collection includes all page artifacts; filter by Subtype (e.g., "Watermark") if only watermarks are needed.

semantic_summary: Shows how to add an image stamp as a centered footer on every page of a PDF document and save the modified file.
rules: Load a PDF document from {input_pdf} using new Document(path).
Create an ImageStamp from {image_path} and configure its layout: set BottomMargin = {int}, HorizontalAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center, VerticalAlignment = VerticalAlignment.Bottom.
Iterate over each {page} in {doc}.Pages and invoke page.AddStamp(imageStamp) to apply the same stamp to all pages.
Save the updated document to {output_pdf} with Document.Save(path).

semantic_summary: Shows how to add an image to the header and a page‑number text fragment to the footer of a PDF document using Aspose.PDF for .NET.
rules: Create a {header} = new Aspose.Pdf.HeaderFooter(); assign it to {page}.Header; add an Aspose.Pdf.Image with its File property set to a {string_literal} path, then add the image to {header}.Paragraphs.

Create a {footer} = new Aspose.Pdf.HeaderFooter(); assign it to {page}.Footer; create a Aspose.Pdf.Text.TextFragment containing the page‑number placeholders (e.g., "Page: ($p of $P ) "), then add the fragment to {footer}.Paragraphs.
After configuring header/footer, call {doc}.Save({output_pdf}); to write the result.
warnings: The placeholders $p and $P are Aspose.PDF specific tokens for current page number and total pages; they must be used inside a TextFragment for automatic substitution.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a PDF document, add a page, and compose a header that contains inline text fragments and an inline image using the Aspose.PDF API.
rules: Instantiate a {doc} via its default constructor, add a {page} with {doc}.Pages.Add(), and assign a new {header_footer} to {page}.Header.

Create a {text_fragment} with desired content, set its visual properties (e.g., {text_fragment}.TextState.ForegroundColor = {color}), and enable inline layout by setting {text_fragment}.IsInLineParagraph = true.

Create an {image}, set its source file via {image}.File = {string_literal}, define its dimensions with {image}.FixWidth = {int} and {image}.FixHeight = {int}, and enable inline layout with {image}.IsInLineParagraph = true.
Add the {text_fragment} and {image} objects to the header/footer by invoking {header_footer}.Paragraphs.Add() in the desired sequence, then save the document with {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The Color type used (Color.Blue, Color.Maroon) may refer to System.Drawing.Color or Aspose.Pdf.Color depending on the project references; the example assumes Aspose.Pdf.Color.

semantic_summary: Shows how to add an image stamp as a header (top‑center) on every page of a PDF document and save the result.
rules: Create an ImageStamp from {string_literal} (image file) and set its positioning properties: TopMargin={int}, HorizontalAlignment={HorizontalAlignment.Center}, VerticalAlignment={VerticalAlignment.Top}.
Iterate over each {page} in {doc}.Pages and invoke {page}.AddStamp({imageStamp}) to apply the same stamp to all pages.
warnings: The same ImageStamp instance is reused for all pages; if per‑page customization is required, instantiate a new ImageStamp inside the loop.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a PageNumberStamp, customize its appearance (format, alignment, font, color), apply it to a specific page of a PDF document, and save the result.
rules: Instantiate a PageNumberStamp, set its Background to {bool}, Format to a string like "Page # of {int}", BottomMargin to {int}, HorizontalAlignment to HorizontalAlignment.{string_literal}, StartingNumber to {int}, then configure its TextState: Font = FontRepository.FindFont({string_literal}), FontSize = {float}, FontStyle = FontStyles.{string_literal} (or combine multiple styles), ForegroundColor = {color}.
Add the configured stamp to a target page by accessing {doc}.Pages[{int}] and calling AddStamp({pageNumberStamp}).
After stamping, persist changes by calling {doc}.Save({string_literal}).
warnings: The example overwrites FontStyle twice (Bold then Italic); only the last assignment takes effect.
Page indices are 1‑based; ensure the requested page exists.
FontRepository.FindFont requires the font to be installed on the executing machine.

semantic_summary: Shows how to add a page‑numbering header or footer to a PDF by placing a FloatingBox on a page and using Aspose.PDF page‑number macros.
rules: Create a new {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document) and add a {page} (Aspose.Pdf.Page) via {doc}.Pages.Add().
Instantiate a {floating_box} (Aspose.Pdf.FloatingBox) with width and height, then set its Left and Top {float} properties to position it on the page.
Add a {text_fragment} (Aspose.Pdf.Text.TextFragment) containing page‑number macros ($p for current page, $P for total pages) to {floating_box}.Paragraphs.
Add the {floating_box} to {page}.Paragraphs so it is rendered as part of the page content (e.g., header/footer).
Save the {doc} to an {output_pdf} path using {doc}.Save({string_literal}).
warnings: Macro syntax ($p, $P) is version‑specific; ensure the Aspose.PDF library version supports these placeholders.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a PDF document, add a header section, and populate it with a formatted table containing text and an image using Aspose.PDF for .NET.
rules: Create a {header} (HeaderFooter) object, assign it to {page}.Header (or .Footer), set its margins, and add content to {header}.Paragraphs collection.
Instantiate a {table} (Table), add it to {header}.Paragraphs, configure DefaultCellBorder and ColumnWidths, then build rows via {table}.Rows.Add() and cells via Row.Cells.Add().
To span a cell across multiple columns, set Cell.ColSpan = {int}.
Set cell background with Cell.BackgroundColor = {color} and text appearance with Cell.DefaultCellTextState.Font = FontRepository.FindFont({string_literal}) and .ForegroundColor = {color}.
Insert an image into a table cell by creating an {image} (Image), setting its File path and optional FixWidth, then adding it to Cell.Paragraphs.
Control text alignment inside a cell using Cell.VerticalAlignment = VerticalAlignment.{enum_value} and Cell.Alignment = HorizontalAlignment.{enum_value}.
warnings: Aspose.Pdf.Color may refer to the Aspose.Pdf specific Color struct; ensure correct namespace if using System.Drawing.Color.
FontRepository.FindFont requires the specified font (e.g., "Helvetica") to be available on the system or embedded resources.

semantic_summary: Shows how to add a centered text footer to every page of an existing PDF by using a TextStamp.
rules: Load a PDF document from {input_pdf} into {doc} using the Document constructor.
Create a TextStamp with {string_literal} and set its BottomMargin to {int}, HorizontalAlignment to Center, and VerticalAlignment to Bottom to define a footer.
Iterate over each {page} in {doc}.Pages and invoke page.AddStamp(textStamp) to apply the footer to all pages.
Save the modified {doc} to {output_pdf}.
warnings: The example reuses a single TextStamp instance for all pages; this is supported but be aware if page‑specific modifications are needed.
Document is not explicitly disposed; rely on garbage collection or wrap in a using statement in production code.

semantic_summary: Shows how to insert a centered text header on all pages of a PDF by creating a TextStamp, configuring its alignment and margin, and adding it to each page.
rules: Create a TextStamp with {string_literal} and configure its layout: set TopMargin = {int}, HorizontalAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center, VerticalAlignment = VerticalAlignment.Top.
Iterate over each {page} in {doc}.Pages and invoke page.AddStamp({text_fragment}) to apply the stamp to all pages.
Persist the changes by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The same TextStamp instance is reused for all pages; modifications after adding to a page will affect previously stamped pages.
TopMargin may need adjustment depending on page size and existing content.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a PDF document, add a table to a page, and embed an image inside a specific table cell using Aspose.PDF for .NET.
rules: Create an {image} object, assign its File property to a {string_literal} path, and embed it in a table cell by invoking cell.Paragraphs.Add({image}).

Add a {table} to a {page} via page.Paragraphs.Add({table}), configure its DefaultCellBorder with new BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {float}) and set ColumnWidths using a space‑separated {string_literal}; then populate rows with table.Rows.Add() and cells with row.Cells.Add(...), optionally adjusting cell properties such as VerticalAlignment.
warnings: ColumnWidths expects a space‑separated string of numeric values; ensure the format matches the table layout requirements.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a table with repeating columns that persist across page breaks, configure automatic column width adjustment, and nest a table inside another table within a PDF document.
rules: To make the first N columns repeat on each page when a table spans multiple pages, set {table}.RepeatingColumnsCount = {int} and ensure {table}.Broken = TableBroken.VerticalInSamePage (or another breaking mode that allows pagination).
For automatic column width fitting based on content, assign {table}.ColumnAdjustment = ColumnAdjustment.AutoFitToContent.
To create a full‑page width table, set {table}.ColumnWidths = "100%" and optionally {table}.HorizontalAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Left.
To nest a table inside another table, add the outer table to {page}.Paragraphs, create a row and cell in the outer table, then add the inner table to that cell's Paragraphs collection.
Populate tables by adding rows via {table}.Rows.Add() and cells via {row}.Cells.Add({string_literal}) for each column.
warnings: The example adds the inner table both inside a cell of the outer table and directly to the page paragraphs; the second addition may be redundant.
RepeatingColumnsCount only takes effect when the table actually breaks across pages; with a small number of rows it may not be observable.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a PDF document, build a two‑column table, insert a text fragment in the first cell and an SVG image in the second cell, then save the result.
rules: To embed an SVG image inside a table cell: create an {image} of type Aspose.Pdf.Image, set {image}.FileType = ImageFileType.Svg, assign {image}.File = {string_literal} (path to .svg), optionally set {image}.FixWidth and {image}.FixHeight ({int}), then add {image} to the target cell's Paragraphs collection.

To construct a simple table: instantiate a {table} (Aspose.Pdf.Table), set {table}.ColumnWidths = {string_literal} (space‑separated widths), add a {row} via {table}.Rows.Add(), add {cell}s with {row}.Cells.Add(), and populate each cell's Paragraphs collection with content such as a {text_fragment} or an {image}.
To place the table on a page: add a new {page} to {doc}.Pages, then add the {table} to {page}.Paragraphs.
warnings: The example uses hard‑coded column width string; dynamic width calculation may be required for different layouts.
FixWidth/FixHeight are optional; omitting them preserves the SVG's intrinsic size.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a Table, configure its borders, populate it with rows and cells, insert it into an existing PDF page, and save the modified document.
rules: Load an existing PDF: {doc} = new Aspose.Pdf.Document({input_pdf});
Instantiate a Table: {table} = new Aspose.Pdf.Table();
Set table border: {table}.Border = new Aspose.Pdf.BorderInfo(Aspose.Pdf.BorderSide.All, {float}, {color});
Set default cell border: {table}.DefaultCellBorder = new Aspose.Pdf.BorderInfo(Aspose.Pdf.BorderSide.All, {float}, {color});
Add rows in a loop: for (int i = {int}; i < {int}; i++) { Row row = {table}.Rows.Add(); row.Cells.Add({string_literal}); ... }
Insert the table into a page: {doc}.Pages[{int}].Paragraphs.Add({table});
Save the updated document: {doc}.Save({output_pdf});
warnings: Page indexing in Aspose.Pdf is 1‑based; ensure the target page exists.
BorderSide.All and Color.FromRgb require System.Drawing.Color conversion, which may need additional using directives.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a PDF document, add a page, insert a table with three columns, set column widths and auto‑fit the table to the page width, customize borders and cell padding, populate rows and cells, and save the resulting PDF.
rules: Instantiate a PDF document and add a page: {doc} = new Document(); {page} = {doc}.Pages.Add();

Create a Table, set column widths via a space‑separated string and enable auto‑fit to window: {table} = new Table(); {table}.ColumnWidths = "{string_literal}"; {table}.ColumnAdjustment = ColumnAdjustment.AutoFitToWindow;

Define default cell border and overall table border using BorderInfo with BorderSide.All and a thickness: {table}.DefaultCellBorder = new BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {float}); {table}.Border = new BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {float});

Configure default cell padding with a MarginInfo object specifying top, left, right, and bottom margins: var margin = new MarginInfo(); margin.Top = {float}; margin.Left = {float}; margin.Right = {float}; margin.Bottom = {float}; {table}.DefaultCellPadding = margin;
Add rows to the table and populate cells with text: var row = {table}.Rows.Add(); row.Cells.Add("{string_literal}"); // repeat for each cell
warnings: ColumnAdjustment.AutoFitToWindow only takes effect when ColumnWidths are explicitly set; otherwise the table may not resize as expected.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a PDF with a table, configure its layout (margins, borders, padding), add rows, and programmatically determine whether the table will overflow the current page by comparing cumulative object heights with the page height.
rules: To check if a table will break, compute {float} pageHeight = {doc}.PageInfo.Height; compute {float} totalObjectsHeight = {page}.PageInfo.Margin.Top + {page}.PageInfo.Margin.Bottom + {table}.Margin.Top + {table}.GetHeight(); if ((pageHeight - totalObjectsHeight) <= {float}) then the next row would cause a page break.

Configure a table's visual layout by setting {table}.ColumnWidths = "{int} {int} {int}"; {table}.DefaultCellBorder = new BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {float}); {table}.Border = new BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {float}); {table}.DefaultCellPadding = new MarginInfo { Top = {float}, Left = {float}, Right = {float}, Bottom = {float} };
Add rows to a table in a loop: for each iteration create {row} = {table}.Rows.Add(); then add cells via {row}.Cells.Add({string_literal}); repeat for each column.
warnings: The height returned by Table.GetHeight() is an estimate and may change after the document is saved or when content wraps.
The threshold value (e.g., 10) used to decide a break is arbitrary and may need adjustment based on font size or row content.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates importing data from an Excel worksheet into a DataTable, creating a PDF document with a table, populating the table from the DataTable, and applying custom styling to header and data rows before saving the PDF.
rules: Create a new {doc}, add a {page} via {doc}.Pages.Add(), instantiate a {table}, and add it to {page}.Paragraphs.

Set {table}.ColumnWidths to a space‑separated string of column widths (e.g., "40 100 100") and assign {table}.DefaultCellBorder = new BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {float}) to apply a uniform border to all cells.

Populate the table from a System.Data.DataTable using {table}.ImportDataTable({data_table}, true, 0, 0, {int}, {int}) where the last two integers represent the total number of rows (including header) and the number of columns.

Iterate over {row}.Cells to customize appearance: set {cell}.BackgroundColor, {cell}.DefaultCellTextState.Font, {cell}.DefaultCellTextState.ForegroundColor, and {cell}.DefaultCellTextState.HorizontalAlignment.
Apply distinct styling to the header row (row index 0) versus data rows (row index >= 1) by using separate loops or conditional logic.
Save the PDF with {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The example relies on Aspose.Cells to read Excel data; this dependency is outside the Aspose.PDF API.
System.Drawing.Color is used for color values; ensure the appropriate namespace is referenced.
ColumnWidths expects a space‑separated string; incorrect formatting may cause runtime errors.
ImportDataTable row count parameter includes the header row; callers must calculate the correct value.

semantic_summary: Shows how to parse a PDF page's content stream, interpret drawing operators to reconstruct border paths, and render those paths to a bitmap image using System.Drawing.
rules: Iterate over {doc}.Pages[{int}].Contents and cast each element to Aspose.Pdf.Operator subclasses to handle specific drawing commands.
Maintain a graphics state stack (push on GSave, pop on GRestore) and update a current transformation matrix (CTM) on ConcatenateMatrix to correctly map PDF coordinates.
Build a System.Drawing.Drawing2D.GraphicsPath by adding lines (MoveTo/LineTo) and rectangles (Re) while tracking the last point, resetting the path on EndPath.

When encountering Stroke, Fill, or EOFill operators, transform the accumulated GraphicsPath with the current CTM and an inversion matrix, then draw or fill it on a System.Drawing.Graphics object using the colors captured from SetRGBColor and SetRGBColorStroke.
warnings: Relies on System.Drawing which is platform‑specific (Windows/.NET Framework); may not work on non‑Windows .NET Core runtimes.
The example manually parses only a subset of PDF operators; other drawing operators (e.g., curves, clipping) are not handled.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a PDF document, add a page, build a table with auto‑fit column adjustment, populate it with a row and cells, and retrieve the calculated table width using the GetWidth method.
rules: Create a {doc} and add a {page}; instantiate a Table (optionally set ColumnAdjustment = ColumnAdjustment.AutoFitToContent), then add at least one Row and populate its Cells before calling Table.GetWidth() to obtain the table's width.
Set Table.ColumnAdjustment to ColumnAdjustment.AutoFitToContent to let column widths be determined by the content of the cells, which directly affects the value returned by GetWidth().
warnings: GetWidth may return a meaningful value only after the table has been laid out (e.g., added to a page or after layout processing). In this isolated example the table is not added to the page, which could lead to default or zero width in some scenarios.

semantic_summary: Shows how to import a DataTable that contains HTML markup into a PDF table, enabling HTML rendering inside table cells.
rules: Create a Table ({table}) and configure its ColumnWidths, Border, DefaultCellBorder, and DefaultCellPadding (using MarginInfo) before adding it to a Document ({doc}).

Fill a System.Data.DataTable with HTML strings and import it into the Table using Table.ImportDataTable({data_table}, {bool includeHeader}, {int startRow}, {int startColumn}, {int rows}, {int columns}, {bool isHtml}=true) to render HTML inside cells.
Add the configured Table to a page via {doc}.Pages[{int pageIndex}].Paragraphs.Add({table}) and then save the document.
warnings: System.Data.DataTable is external to Aspose.PDF and not listed in api_surface.
Assumed BorderSide enum resides in Aspose.Pdf namespace; if the namespace differs, adjust accordingly.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a table with borders, populate it with rows and cells, and force a page break after a configurable number of rows by setting the Row.IsInNewPage property.
rules: When building a table, instantiate Aspose.Pdf.Table, assign Border and DefaultCellBorder using new BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {color}), set ColumnWidths via a space‑separated {string_literal}, then add Aspose.Pdf.Row objects to Table.Rows.
To insert a page break after every N rows, evaluate the row index ({int}) and set the corresponding Row.IsInNewPage = true before the table is added to the page's Paragraphs collection.
warnings: Row.IsInNewPage only takes effect after the table is added to a page; setting it earlier may be ignored.
ColumnWidths expects a space‑separated string of numeric values; invalid formats will raise an exception.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a PDF document, build a table from a System.Data.DataTable, configure its appearance, import the data into the table, and save the resulting PDF.
rules: Create a {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document), add a {page} via {doc}.Pages.Add(), instantiate a Table, set Table.ColumnWidths to a {string_literal}, configure Table.Border and Table.DefaultCellBorder with new BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {float}, {color}), then import data using Table.ImportDataTable({data_table}, {bool} includeHeader, {int} startRow, {int} startColumn, {int} rows, {int} columns).
Add the prepared Table to the target page by calling {doc}.Pages[1].Paragraphs.Add(table) and persist the PDF with {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to locate a table in a PDF, access a specific text fragment within a cell, modify its text, and save the updated document.
rules: Load {input_pdf} into a {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document).
Instantiate a TableAbsorber, then call absorber.Visit({page}) on the target page to populate absorber.TableList.
Navigate to a specific cell via absorber.TableList[{int}].RowList[{int}].CellList[{int}] and retrieve a {text_fragment} from its TextFragments collection.
Assign a new string to {text_fragment}.Text to modify the cell content.
Save the modified {doc} to {output_pdf} using Document.Save.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a table, configure its borders, cell padding, column widths, add rows and cells, insert a TextFragment into a cell, control word‑wrap, and save the PDF.

Set column widths with {table}.ColumnWidths = "{float} {float} {float}" (space‑separated values).

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to locate and delete a table from a specific page of a PDF using Aspose.PDF's TableAbsorber.

Retrieve a target table from TableAbsorber.TableList[{int}] (e.g., the first table) and assign it to {table}.
Remove the selected table from the document using TableAbsorber.Remove({table}).

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a PDF document, configure page margins and orientation, build tables with rows, cells and text fragments, add multiple tables to a page, and force a table onto a new page using the IsInNewPage property.

Add a table to a page: {page}.Paragraphs.Add({table});
Force a table onto a new page by setting {table}.IsInNewPage = true before adding it to the page's Paragraphs collection.

rules: Load a PDF document from {input_pdf} into {doc}.

Retrieve an existing table via absorber.TableList[{int}] and assign to {absorbed_table}.
Instantiate a new Table, set ColumnWidths to {string_literal}, and configure DefaultCellBorder with BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {float}).
