warnings: The example uses System.Drawing.Color for color creation, which is not part of Aspose.Pdf API.
Hard‑coded dimensions and positions (width, height, Left, Top) may need adjustment for different page sizes.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a simple PDF document, add a page with a text fragment, and save it; includes a commented example of converting the document to PDF/A‑1A format.
rules: Instantiate a new {doc} via new Aspose.Pdf.Document().
Add a new page to {doc} with {doc}.Pages.Add() and obtain a {page} reference.
Add textual content to a {page} by creating a {text_fragment} (new TextFragment({string_literal})) and adding it to {page}.Paragraphs.
Persist the PDF either to a stream using {doc}.Save({output_stream}) or directly to a file path using {doc}.Save({string_literal}).
To convert an existing PDF to PDF/A‑1A, call {doc}.Convert(sourceStream, PdfFormat.PDF_A_1A, ConvertErrorAction.Delete).
warnings: The PDF/A conversion call is commented out, so the example does not actually produce a PDF/A‑1A file.
The Convert method expects an input stream containing a PDF; using a newly created document may require saving to a stream first.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to insert a Table of Contents page into an existing PDF, customize its title and page‑number prefix, and generate TOC entries that link to each document page.
rules: Insert a new page at position {int} using {doc}.Pages.Insert({int}) and assign a TocInfo instance to the page's TocInfo property.
Create a TextFragment {text_fragment} for the TOC title, set its TextState.FontSize and TextState.FontStyle, then assign it to TocInfo.Title.
Set a custom page‑number prefix by assigning a string literal to TocInfo.PageNumbersPrefix.

For each source page, instantiate a Heading with level {int}, set Heading.TocPage = {toc_page}, add a TextSegment containing the entry label, set Heading.DestinationPage = {page}, set Heading.Top = {page}.Rect.Height, and add the heading to {toc_page}.Paragraphs.
warnings: The loop uses doc.Pages[i + 1] after inserting a new page, which shifts page indices; ensure the index logic matches the intended source pages.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to attach a custom progress handler to a DocSaveOptions object to receive conversion progress events while saving a PDF document.
rules: When saving a PDF, assign a delegate to {doc}.SaveOptions.CustomProgressHandler using the UnifiedSaveOptions.ConversionProgressEventHandler signature to receive progress callbacks.

The progress callback method must accept a DocSaveOptions.ProgressEventHandlerInfo parameter and can inspect eventInfo.EventType (ProgressEventType) along with eventInfo.Value and eventInfo.MaxValue to determine the current stage and percentage.

Invoke Document.Save({output_pdf}, {saveOptions}) after configuring the custom handler; the handler will be invoked for events such as TotalProgress, SourcePageAnalysed, ResultPageCreated, and ResultPageSaved.

semantic_summary: Shows how to embed all fonts used in a PDF document, including fonts inside page resources and form XObjects, by setting the IsEmbedded property on each Font object.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} and iterate over each {page} in {doc}.Pages.
If {page}.Resources.Fonts is not null, iterate each {font} and set {font}.IsEmbedded = true when {font}.IsEmbedded is false.

If {page}.Resources.Forms is not null, iterate each {form} in {page}.Resources.Forms; for each {form}, if {form}.Resources.Fonts is not null, iterate each {font} and set {font}.IsEmbedded = true when {font}.IsEmbedded is false.
warnings: XForm class is defined in Aspose.Pdf.Annotations; ensure the appropriate namespace is imported.
Setting IsEmbedded only works for fonts that are available for embedding; already embedded fonts are left unchanged.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to embed a TrueType font (e.g., Arial) into a PDF while creating the document programmatically using Aspose.PDF for .NET.
rules: When creating a new PDF, instantiate {doc}=Aspose.Pdf.Document and add a page via {page}=doc.Pages.Add().
To embed a font, obtain a Font object with FontRepository.FindFont("{font_name}") and set its IsEmbedded property to true on a TextState, then assign that TextState to a TextSegment.
Compose text by creating a TextFragment, adding one or more TextSegment objects (with the configured TextState) to its Segments collection, and then adding the fragment to {page}.Paragraphs.
Save the resulting document with doc.Save("{output_pdf}") to persist the embedded font.
warnings: The example assumes the specified font (e.g., Arial) is installed and discoverable by FontRepository on the host machine.
The output directory must exist; otherwise doc.Save may throw an exception.

semantic_summary: Shows how to embed fonts as subsets in a PDF document by applying different FontSubsetStrategy options before saving the file.
rules: To embed all fonts as subsets, invoke {doc}.FontUtilities.SubsetFonts(FontSubsetStrategy.SubsetAllFonts) before calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
To embed only fonts that are already embedded as subsets, invoke {doc}.FontUtilities.SubsetFonts(FontSubsetStrategy.SubsetEmbeddedFontsOnly) before calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The exact type name of the FontUtilities property is inferred; if the library version differs, the fully qualified name may vary.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to flatten all annotations in a PDF document so they become part of the page content and are no longer editable, then saves the modified document.
rules: Iterate over each {page} in {doc}.Pages and then over each {annotation} in {page}.Annotations, invoking {annotation}.Flatten() to convert the annotation into static page content.
After processing annotations, persist the changes by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The exact fully qualified type for Annotation is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.Annotations.Annotation; verify if a different namespace is used in the specific version.
Flattening is irreversible within the same document instance; ensure a backup if original editable annotations are needed.

semantic_summary: Shows how to load a PDF document and retrieve the collection of fonts that are used within it.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} via new Document({input_pdf}) and then call {doc}.FontUtilities.GetAllFonts() to obtain an array of {font}.
Iterate over the returned {font}[] and access properties such as {font}.FontName for each font.
warnings: GetAllFonts returns only fonts referenced in the document; it does not list system‑available fonts.
The example assumes the PDF file exists at the specified path.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to read the PDF viewer‑preference settings (window positioning, layout, and display options) from an existing PDF file using Aspose.Pdf.Document properties.
rules: Instantiate a {input_pdf} as an Aspose.Pdf.Document to gain access to viewer‑preference properties.
Read boolean viewer flags (CenterWindow, DisplayDocTitle, FitWindow, HideMenubar, HideToolBar, HideWindowUI) directly from the Document instance.
Read enumerated viewer settings (Direction, NonFullScreenPageMode, PageLayout, PageMode) directly from the Document instance.

semantic_summary: Shows how to open a PDF file with Aspose.PDF and retrieve its built‑in metadata (author, title, dates, keywords, etc.) via the DocumentInfo object.
rules: Instantiate a {doc} by loading an {input_pdf} file: new Document({input_pdf}).
Obtain the metadata container {docInfo} via {doc}.Info and read its properties (Author, CreationDate, Keywords, ModDate, Subject, Title).
warnings: DocumentInfo properties may be null if the corresponding metadata is not present in the source PDF.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to obtain basic metadata (subject, title, keywords, creator, creation/modification dates) and validation flags (IsPdfFile, IsEncrypted) from a PDF file using Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileInfo.
rules: Instantiate PdfFileInfo with {input_pdf} to load a PDF document for metadata extraction.
Read metadata properties such as Subject, Title, Keywords, Creator, CreationDate, and ModDate from the PdfFileInfo instance.
Use the IsPdfFile and IsEncrypted boolean properties of PdfFileInfo to verify that the file is a valid PDF and to check its encryption status.
warnings: PdfFileInfo in this example is constructed with a file path; constructing it from a stream is not shown.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to subscribe to the FontSubstitution event of a Document to receive notifications whenever a font is replaced during PDF loading.
rules: Attach a handler to {doc}.FontSubstitution event using: {doc}.FontSubstitution += new Document.FontSubstitutionHandler({handler_method});
Define {handler_method} with signature (Aspose.Pdf.Text.Font {old_font}, Aspose.Pdf.Text.Font {new_font}) to process or log font substitution details.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to open a PDF document and read XMP metadata values using the Document.Metadata dictionary.
rules: Load a PDF file into a {doc} instance: {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});
Read an XMP metadata entry by key: var value = {doc}.Metadata["xmp:<property_name>"];
warnings: The exact type of Document.Metadata is not specified; it behaves like a dictionary of string keys to string values.
The example assumes the PDF contains the specified XMP properties; missing keys will return null or throw.

semantic_summary: Shows how to extract XMP metadata from a PDF file using the PdfXmpMetadata class, including both standard and custom metadata fields.
rules: Instantiate a PdfXmpMetadata object and call BindPdf({input_pdf}) before accessing any metadata values.
Access XMP metadata via the indexer: use DefaultMetadataProperties enum members for standard fields (e.g., CreateDate, MetadataDate) and a string key with a namespace prefix for custom fields.
warnings: Custom namespace keys must exist in the PDF's XMP packet; otherwise the indexer may return null.

semantic_summary: Shows how to obtain the initial view zoom factor of a PDF by reading the Zoom value from the document's OpenAction destination.
rules: Load {doc} from {input_pdf} using the Document constructor.
Retrieve the document's OpenAction, cast it to a GoToAction, then cast its Destination to XYZExplicitDestination and read the Zoom property.
warnings: OpenAction may be null or not a GoToAction; the cast can fail.
Destination may not be an XYZExplicitDestination, in which case the Zoom property is unavailable.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a Table of Contents page in a PDF, customize its entry formatting, and hide the page numbers for the TOC entries using Aspose.PDF for .NET.
rules: Create a TOC page by adding a new {page} to {doc}.Pages, instantiate a {tocInfo} (Aspose.Pdf.TocInfo), assign it to {page}.TocInfo, and optionally set a title using a {textFragment} placed in tocInfo.Title.
Hide page numbers in the generated TOC by setting tocInfo.IsShowPageNumbers = false.

Customize per‑level TOC entry appearance by setting tocInfo.FormatArrayLength = {int} and then configuring tocInfo.FormatArray[{int}].Margin and tocInfo.FormatArray[{int}].TextState properties such as FontStyle, FontSize, Underline, etc.

Add headings that will appear in the TOC by creating a new Heading({int} level), setting heading.TocPage = {tocPage}, heading.IsInList = true, heading.IsAutoSequence = true, adding a TextSegment with the desired text, and finally adding the heading to a {page}.Paragraphs collection.
warnings: The example relies on TocInfo.FormatArray being automatically allocated after setting FormatArrayLength; in some versions explicit initialization may be required.
Properties Heading.IsInList and Heading.IsAutoSequence control TOC entry generation and may behave differently across Aspose.PDF versions.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to enable linking of duplicate streams during PDF optimization by configuring OptimizationOptions and invoking OptimizeResources on a Document, then saving the optimized PDF.
rules: Set {bool} true on OptimizationOptions.LinkDuplcateStreams (or LinkDuplicateStreams) to instruct the optimizer to link duplicate streams instead of keeping separate copies.
Call {doc}.OptimizeResources({optimizeOptions}) after opening the PDF to apply the specified optimization settings, then save the document with {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The property name in the code is 'LinkDuplcateStreams' which appears to be a typo; the correct Aspose.PDF property is likely 'LinkDuplicateStreams'.
The example relies on external helper RunExamples.GetDataDir_AsposePdf_WorkingDocuments() for path resolution, which is not part of the Aspose.PDF API.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load an existing PDF, apply the built‑in web optimization routine, and save the optimized file.
rules: Load a PDF document from a file path using the Document(string) constructor: {input_pdf}
Invoke Document.Optimize() to reduce file size and prepare the document for web delivery.
Save the optimized document to a target path using Document.Save(string): {output_pdf}

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to shrink an existing PDF by configuring OptimizationOptions to link duplicate streams, remove unused objects/streams, and compress images, then applying OptimizeResources and saving the optimized document.
rules: Load a PDF into {doc} with: Document {doc} = new Document({input_pdf});

Create an OptimizationOptions instance and set: LinkDuplcateStreams = {bool}, RemoveUnusedObjects = {bool}, RemoveUnusedStreams = {bool}, ImageCompressionOptions.CompressImages = {bool}, ImageCompressionOptions.ImageQuality = {int};
Apply the options via {doc}.OptimizeResources({optimizationOptions}) and then persist the result with {doc}.Save({output_pdf});
warnings: Ensure ImageCompressionOptions is instantiated (some SDK versions may require explicit creation) before setting its properties.

semantic_summary: Shows how to insert a page break into a PDF using Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor.AddPageBreak with overloads for Document objects, file paths, and streams.
rules: Instantiate a PdfFileEditor and call AddPageBreak(source, destination, new PdfFileEditor.PageBreak[] { new PdfFileEditor.PageBreak({page}, {float}) }) where source/destination can be Document objects, file path strings, or Stream objects.
When using Document objects, create an empty Document for the destination, invoke AddPageBreak, then persist the result with destination.Save({output_pdf}).
When using Stream overloads, open source and destination streams with appropriate FileMode/FileAccess, invoke AddPageBreak, and close both streams after the operation.
warnings: PdfFileEditor.PageBreak is a nested type; its exact fully‑qualified name is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileEditor.PageBreak.
Page numbers are 1‑based; providing an invalid page index will raise an exception.
A valid Aspose license is required for execution.

semantic_summary: Shows how to remove unused objects from a PDF by configuring OptimizationOptions and invoking OptimizeResources, then saving the optimized document.
rules: Create an {OptimizationOptions} instance, set its RemoveUnusedObjects property to {bool:true}, and pass it to {doc}.OptimizeResources to purge unused objects from the PDF.
After optimization, persist the changes by calling {doc}.Save with the desired {output_pdf} path.
warnings: The example assumes the Aspose.Pdf namespace is referenced and that OptimizeResources accepts an OptimizationOptions object; method signatures may differ in older library versions.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to remove unused streams from a PDF by configuring optimization options and invoking the OptimizeResources method before saving the document.
rules: Load {input_pdf} into a Document instance, create an OptimizationOptions object with RemoveUnusedStreams = {bool:true}, and call Document.OptimizeResources(options) to purge unused streams.
After optimization, call Document.Save({output_pdf}) to persist the cleaned PDF.
warnings: The fully qualified namespace for OptimizationOptions is inferred as Aspose.Pdf.Pdf.Optimization.OptimizationOptions; verify if the actual namespace differs.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to specify a default fallback font when saving a PDF that contains missing fonts by using PdfSaveOptions.DefaultFontName.
rules: Create a {doc} from {input_pdf} using a FileStream.
Set {string_literal} as PdfSaveOptions.DefaultFontName to define a fallback font, then save the {doc} to {output_pdf} with those options.
warnings: Assumed fully qualified type names for PdfSaveOptions; verify namespace if different.

semantic_summary: The example opens an existing PDF, configures various document‑window and viewer display properties (such as centering the window, reading direction, UI visibility, page layout and mode), and saves the modified PDF.
rules: Load a PDF into a {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document) from {input_pdf}, set any combination of the boolean window properties (CenterWindow, FitWindow, HideMenubar, HideToolBar, HideWindowUI, DisplayDocTitle) to {bool}, then call {doc}.Save({output_pdf}) to persist the changes.
Set the reading order of the document by assigning {doc}.Direction = Direction.{R2L|L2R}, where Direction is an Aspose.Pdf.Direction enum value.

Control the initial viewer layout and mode by setting {doc}.PageLayout = PageLayout.{enum_value} and {doc}.PageMode = PageMode.{enum_value}; optionally set {doc}.NonFullScreenPageMode = PageMode.{enum_value} for behavior after exiting full‑screen.
warnings: The example assumes the input PDF exists at the specified path; error handling for missing files is not shown.
Saving overwrites the file at {output_pdf} if it already exists.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a PDF, add a page with text, embed JavaScript as an open action to enforce an expiry date, and save the document.
rules: Assign a JavaScript open action to a PDF: {doc}.OpenAction = new JavascriptAction({string_literal});

Create a simple PDF by instantiating Document, adding a page via {doc}.Pages.Add(), and inserting a TextFragment into the first page: {doc}.Pages[1].Paragraphs.Add(new TextFragment({string_literal}));
Persist the PDF to a file using {doc}.Save({string_literal});
warnings: Aspose.Pdf uses 1‑based indexing for Pages collection; accessing Pages[1] assumes at least one page has been added.
The JavaScript code is provided as a raw string; special characters may need escaping in C# literals.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to set or update PDF document metadata (author, title, subject, keywords, creation and modification dates) using Aspose.Pdf's DocumentInfo class and then save the updated file.
rules: Load {input_pdf} into a {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document), instantiate a DocumentInfo with that {doc}, assign {string_literal} values to metadata properties (Author, Title, Subject, Keywords) and DateTime values to CreationDate and ModDate, then save the {doc} to {output_pdf}.
DocumentInfo must be created after the Document is loaded; any changes to its properties are persisted only when the owning Document is saved.
warnings: DateTime values are set directly (DateTime.Now) which does not map to an existing placeholder; the rule uses generic description for date properties.

semantic_summary: Shows how to modify PDF metadata such as author, title, keywords, and creator using the Aspose.Pdf.Facades.PdfFileInfo class and save the updated information to a new PDF file.
rules: Instantiate PdfFileInfo with the path to an existing PDF ({input_pdf}) to access its metadata.
Set metadata properties (Author, Title, Keywords, Creator) on the PdfFileInfo instance using string literals ({string_literal}).
Call SaveNewInfo with the desired output path ({output_pdf}) to write a new PDF containing the updated metadata.
warnings: PdfFileInfo belongs to the Aspose.Pdf.Facades namespace; it updates only document information, not page content.
SaveNewInfo creates a new file; the original PDF remains unchanged.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to preset duplex printing options for the PDF print dialog using both the Document API and the PdfContentEditor façade to modify viewer preferences of an existing PDF.
rules: To set a default duplex printing mode for a new PDF, create a {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document), add required pages, assign {doc}.Duplex = PrintDuplex.{DuplexMode}, then save the document.

To modify the print dialog settings of an existing PDF, bind the file with a {PdfContentEditor}, optionally query current settings via GetViewerPreference() & ViewerPreference.{Flag}, then apply a new setting with ChangeViewerPreference(ViewerPreference.{Flag}) and save.
warnings: The example relies on external helper RunExamples.GetDataDir_* to resolve file paths, which is not shown.
ViewerPreference flags are combined using bitwise operations; changing a flag may overwrite other existing viewer preferences if not merged.

semantic_summary: Shows how to modify a PDF's viewer preferences (e.g., window centering, menu bar visibility, page mode) using the PdfContentEditor facade.
rules: Instantiate {class:PdfContentEditor}, call BindPdf({input_pdf}) to load the document, then invoke ChangeViewerPreference({viewer_preference}) for each desired preference, and finally Save({output_pdf}).
ViewerPreference values are provided by the Aspose.Pdf.Facades.ViewerPreference enum; multiple calls can be made to set several flags before saving.

semantic_summary: Shows how to add or modify XMP metadata entries in a PDF document and how to register a custom namespace prefix before setting prefixed metadata.
rules: Open a PDF with {input_pdf} using new Document({input_pdf}); then set XMP entries via pdfDocument.Metadata["xmp:{string_literal}"] = {value}; finally save to {output_pdf} with pdfDocument.Save({output_pdf});

Before using a custom XMP prefix, register its namespace URI with pdfDocument.Metadata.RegisterNamespaceUri("{string_literal}", "{string_literal}"); then you can assign metadata using the registered prefix.
warnings: The exact class name for the Metadata property is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.DocumentMetadata; if the library uses a different type name, adjust accordingly.

semantic_summary: Shows how to bind a PDF to a PdfXmpMetadata object, manipulate standard and custom XMP metadata properties (add, modify, remove), register a custom namespace, and save the updated metadata back to a PDF.
rules: Bind a PDF file to a PdfXmpMetadata instance using BindPdf({input_pdf}) before performing any metadata operations.
Add a standard XMP property with Add({metadata_key}, {string_literal}) or set/replace it via the indexer: xmpMetaData[{metadata_key}] = {string_literal}.
Remove an existing XMP property using Remove({metadata_key}).
Register a custom namespace before adding custom properties: RegisterNamespaceURI({custom_namespace_prefix}, {custom_namespace_uri}).

Add or modify a custom property using the fully qualified name with the registered prefix: Add({custom_namespace_prefix}:{custom_property_name}, {custom_property_value}) or xmpMetaData[{custom_namespace_prefix}:{custom_property_name}] = {custom_property_value}.
Persist changes to a new PDF file with Save({output_pdf}) and release resources with Close().
warnings: DefaultMetadataProperties is referenced as a static class/enum; exact type not verified.
Method signatures (e.g., RegisterNamespaceURI) assume string parameters for prefix and URI.

semantic_summary: Shows how to define an initial zoom level for a PDF by creating an XYZExplicitDestination, wrapping it in a GoToAction, assigning it to the document's OpenAction, and saving the modified file.
rules: Create an XYZExplicitDestination with {int} pageNumber, {float} left, {float} top, and {float} zoomFactor, then wrap it in a GoToAction and assign to {doc}.OpenAction to set the initial view zoom.
After configuring OpenAction, invoke {doc}.Save({output_pdf}) to persist the zoom setting.
warnings: XYZExplicitDestination expects a 1‑based page number; ensure the correct indexing when specifying the page.

semantic_summary: Shows how to load a PDF, attempt to reduce its size using OptimizeResources, and save the optimized document.
rules: Load a PDF document from {input_pdf} by creating a new Document instance.
Call OptimizeResources() on the Document to attempt shrinking the PDF.
Save the (potentially) optimized document to {output_pdf} using Document.Save(string).
warnings: OptimizeResources() does not guarantee that the document size will be reduced.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to trim white margins around a PDF page by rendering the page to an image, detecting the non‑white pixel bounds, and updating the page's CropBox accordingly.
rules: Render a PDF {page} to an image stream using a device: {device}=new PngDevice(new Resolution({int} dpi)); {device}.Process({page}, {stream});
Lock the bitmap bits and scan each row/column to locate the first and last non‑white pixels (where any channel != 255) to compute {int} leftNonWhite, rightNonWhite, topNonWhite, bottomNonWhite.

Adjust the page's CropBox based on the detected bounds and the original CropBox: {page}.CropBox = new Aspose.Pdf.Rectangle(leftNonWhite + prevCropBox.LLX, (height + prevCropBox.LLY) - bottomNonWhite, rightNonWhite + prevCropBox.LLX, (height + prevCropBox.LLY) - topNonWhite);
warnings: Relies on System.Drawing and assumes a 32bppRgb pixel format; may not work on non‑Windows platforms.
White detection is hard‑coded to RGB 255,255,255 and does not consider anti‑aliasing or other background colors.
CropBox calculation assumes no page rotation or scaling beyond the rendered image dimensions.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to remove embedded font data from a PDF by using Aspose.PDF's optimization options, thereby reducing the file size.
rules: Load {input_pdf} into a Document instance, create an OptimizationOptions object with UnembedFonts = true, and invoke Document.OptimizeResources(options) to unembed fonts.
Save the optimized Document to {output_pdf} using Document.Save(path).
warnings: Assumed fully qualified name for OptimizationOptions as Aspose.Pdf.Optimization.OptimizationOptions; verify if namespace differs in the library version.

semantic_summary: Shows how to load a PDF file and validate it against the PDF/A‑1a standard, writing the validation report to an XML file.
rules: Load a PDF document from a file path using the Document(string filePath) constructor: {input_pdf} → new Document({string_literal})

Validate the loaded document against a specific PDF/A conformance level by calling Document.Validate(outputPath, PdfFormat.{pdfa_standard}) where {pdfa_standard} is a member of the PdfFormat enum (e.g., PDF_A_1A).

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load a PDF file and validate it against a PDF/A compliance level, outputting the validation results to an XML file.
rules: Load a PDF document using `new Document({input_pdf})` before performing any validation.
Call `Document.Validate({output_xml}, PdfFormat.{pdf_format})` to validate the PDF against a specific PDF/A standard; the result is written to the specified XML file.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to load a PDF file and validate it against the PDF/UA 1.0 standard, outputting the validation report to an XML file.
rules: Instantiate a {input_pdf} as an Aspose.Pdf.Document using its file path constructor.
Call Document.Validate({output_xml}, {pdf_standard}) to validate the document against a specific PDF standard (e.g., PdfFormat.PDF_UA_1) and write the result to an XML file.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to generate a PDF document by binding an XML data source and an XSLT stylesheet to an empty Aspose.Pdf.Document and then saving the result.
rules: Create a new {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document) instance, then call {doc}.BindXml({string_literal} xmlPath, {string_literal} xsltPath) to populate the document from XML/XSLT.
After binding, invoke {doc}.Save({string_literal} outputPath) to write the generated PDF to disk.
warnings: BindXml expects valid file paths to existing XML and XSLT files; missing files will raise an exception.
The example creates an empty document before binding; binding after adding pages may produce unexpected layout.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to generate a PDF document by binding an XML source file with an XSLT stylesheet using Aspose.PDF, and then saving the resulting PDF.
rules: Create a new {doc} = new Aspose.Pdf.Document();
Bind an XML file and an XSLT stylesheet to the document using {doc}.BindXml({string_literal} xmlPath, {string_literal} xsltPath);
Persist the transformed PDF with {doc}.Save({string_literal} outputPath);
warnings: BindXml requires that the provided XML and XSLT files are well‑formed and compatible; otherwise an exception will be thrown.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to traverse a tagged PDF's structure tree, read properties of structure elements, modify those properties on specific child elements, and save the updated document.
rules: To read properties of tagged structure elements: obtain {doc}.TaggedContent.StructTreeRootElement.ChildElements, iterate over each {element} in the collection, cast to StructureElement, then access Title, Language, ActualText, ExpansionText, and AlternativeText.

To modify properties of a specific child element: navigate to the desired element via {doc}.TaggedContent.RootElement.ChildElements[{int}].ChildElements, cast each to StructureElement, set Title, Language, ActualText, ExpansionText, and AlternativeText as needed, then call {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The example assumes the PDF is already tagged and that a child element exists at index 1; runtime checks may be required.
StructureElement properties are optional; setting them may have no visual effect if the PDF viewer does not use them.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a new tagged PDF, define document metadata, build a hierarchy of paragraph and span structure elements (including nested spans), save the PDF and validate it for PDF/UA compliance.
rules: Create a new {doc} via new Document() and obtain its tagged content with {doc}.TaggedContent.
Set document-level metadata on the tagged content using SetTitle({string_literal}) and SetLanguage({string_literal}).
Create structural elements through the tagged content factory methods: CreateParagraphElement() returns a {ParagraphElement}, CreateSpanElement() returns a {SpanElement}.
Assign textual content to a span or paragraph using SetText({string_literal}).
Build the logical structure by appending child elements: use AppendChild(child) on a {StructureElement} or {ParagraphElement} to add {SpanElement} or nested {SpanElement} objects.
Nested spans can be created by appending a {SpanElement} to another {SpanElement} before attaching the parent span to a paragraph.
Save the tagged PDF with Document.Save({output_pdf}).
Validate PDF/UA compliance by reloading the saved file into a {doc} and calling Validate({log_file}, PdfFormat.PDF_UA_1).
warnings: Namespace for ParagraphElement and SpanElement assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.Tagged; verify against actual library version.
SetText on ParagraphElement may affect the paragraph's own text content separate from its child spans; ordering of SetText and AppendChild may influence final output.

semantic_summary: The example shows how to create Note structure elements inside a tagged PDF, assign unique IDs, and validate the resulting document for PDF/UA compliance.
rules: To add a note element, retrieve ITaggedContent from {doc}, create a ParagraphElement via CreateParagraphElement(), append it to the RootElement, then create a NoteElement via CreateNoteElement() and append it to the paragraph.
Assign a unique identifier to each structure element using SetId({string_literal}); attempting to reuse an existing ID throws TaggedException.
Clearing the ID of a note element with ClearId() may cause the document to fail PDF/UA compliance validation.
After modifying the tagged structure, persist changes with {doc}.Save({output_pdf}) and verify compliance using {doc}.Validate({logFile}, PdfFormat.PDF_UA_1).
warnings: The exact namespace for PdfFormat enum is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.PdfFormat; verify in the used version.
RunExamples.GetDataDir_AsposePdf_WorkingDocuments() is a helper not part of Aspose.PDF API.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a new PDF, enable tagging, set document metadata, add a tagged image (figure) with alternative text and title, and save the result.
rules: When creating a tagged PDF, retrieve the ITaggedContent from {doc} and set document metadata using SetTitle({string_literal}) and SetLanguage({string_literal}) before adding any tagged elements.

To embed a tagged image, call ITaggedContent.CreateFigureElement() to obtain an IllustrationElement, set its AlternativeText, Title, and optional tag via SetTag({string_literal}), assign the image source with SetImage({string_literal}), then append the element to ITaggedContent.RootElement using AppendChild.
After constructing the tagged content hierarchy, persist the PDF by invoking {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: Exact fully qualified names for IllustrationElement and IElement are inferred from namespace imports; verify against the Aspose.PDF version used.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a new PDF document, enable tagged PDF support, set document title and language, add block‑level structure elements (header and paragraphs) with actual text, and save the result.
rules: To initialize a tagged PDF, create a {doc} (Aspose.Pdf.Document) and retrieve its ITaggedContent via {doc}.TaggedContent.
Set document metadata for accessibility using ITaggedContent.SetTitle({string_literal}) and ITaggedContent.SetLanguage({string_literal}) before adding any structure elements.

Create block‑level structure elements through ITaggedContent.CreateHeaderElement() or ITaggedContent.CreateParagraphElement(), assign their ActualText property, and (optionally) attach them to the document's logical structure.
After constructing the tagged content, persist the PDF with {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The example creates HeaderElement and ParagraphElement instances but does not explicitly add them to a page or the document's structure tree; in a real scenario you would need to associate them with page content.
The exact namespace of ITaggedContent is inferred; if the type resides in a different namespace, adjust the import accordingly.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a new PDF document, enable tagging, set document metadata, instantiate a wide range of logical structure elements via ITaggedContent, and save the tagged PDF.
rules: To create a tagged PDF, instantiate {doc}=new Document() and obtain its tagging interface via {tagged}= {doc}.TaggedContent.
Set document-level tagging metadata using {tagged}.SetTitle({string_literal}) and {tagged}.SetLanguage({string_literal}).
Create logical structure elements by calling the appropriate CreateXElement method on {tagged}, e.g., {tagged}.CreateParagraphElement() or {tagged}.CreateHeaderElement({int} level).
After constructing the required structure (elements may be attached to pages or other elements as needed), persist the PDF with {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The example creates structure element objects but does not demonstrate attaching them to pages or other elements; additional steps are required to integrate them into the PDF's logical structure.
Some CreateXElement methods are noted as "under development" and may have limited functionality in certain library versions.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a new PDF, enable tagging, set document metadata, build a hierarchical logical structure using Sect, Div, and Art elements, and save the tagged PDF.
rules: To build a logical structure tree in a tagged PDF, retrieve the ITaggedContent from {doc}, create logical elements via ITaggedContent.Create{Element} (e.g., SectElement, DivElement, ArtElement), and attach them to parent elements using StructureElement.AppendChild, starting with ITaggedContent.RootElement as the root.
Set document metadata for a tagged PDF before constructing the structure by calling ITaggedContent.SetTitle({string_literal}) and ITaggedContent.SetLanguage({string_literal}).
warnings: Assumed fully qualified names for element classes (SectElement, DivElement, ArtElement) are in Aspose.Pdf.Tagged namespace; verify against the library version.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a tagged PDF containing a styled table with header, body, and footer rows, apply table-level attributes, save the document, and validate PDF/UA compliance.
rules: To create a tagged PDF, instantiate {doc}=new Document(), obtain {tagged}=doc.TaggedContent, set document metadata via tagged.SetTitle({string_literal}) and tagged.SetLanguage({string_literal}), retrieve the root element with tagged.RootElement, create a table with tagged.CreateTableElement(), and append it to the root using rootElement.AppendChild(tableElement).

Build table sections by calling tableElement.CreateTHead(), CreateTBody(), and CreateTFoot() to get {thead}, {tbody}, {tfoot}. For each section, add rows with section.CreateTR(), set row properties (e.g., AlternativeText, BackgroundColor), then add cells: header cells via row.CreateTH() and body/footer cells via row.CreateTD(). Configure cell appearance (SetText, BackgroundColor, Border, Margin, Alignment, ColSpan, RowSpan, DefaultCellTextState, IsWordWrapped, VerticalAlignment).

Assign table-level attributes by retrieving the attribute collection with tableElement.Attributes.GetAttributes(AttributeOwnerStandard.Table), creating a new StructureAttribute with a specific AttributeKey (e.g., Summary), setting its value via SetStringValue({string_literal}), and adding it to the collection with attributes.SetAttribute(attribute).

Validate PDF/UA compliance after saving: reload the document with new Document({output_pdf}), then call doc.Validate({string_literal}, PdfFormat.PDF_UA_1) which returns a {bool} indicating compliance.
warnings: Namespace for Color, BorderInfo, and related enums is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf; verify if they belong to Aspose.Pdf or a sub‑namespace.
The exact overload signatures for SetTitle, SetLanguage, and Validate are inferred from usage; confirm parameter types if needed.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a tagged PDF, set document metadata, build a custom logical structure hierarchy with custom tag names, and add text content before saving.
rules: To configure a tagged PDF, retrieve {doc}.TaggedContent as {taggedContent} and call {taggedContent}.SetTitle({string_literal}) and {taggedContent}.SetLanguage({string_literal}).
Create logical structure elements via {taggedContent}.CreateSectElement(), CreateParagraphElement(), CreateSpanElement() and assemble them using AppendChild(parent, child) to form the hierarchy.
Assign a custom tag name to any logical structure element by invoking SetTag({string_literal}) on the element.
Add textual content to paragraph or span elements with SetText({string_literal}) before saving the document.
warnings: Assumed full namespaces for ParagraphElement and SpanElement as Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure; verify if they belong to a different sub‑namespace.
Method SetTag is assumed to exist on the logical structure element base class.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a tagged PDF, set its title and language, and add a figure illustration element with alternative text, title, tag, and image.
rules: To set document metadata for a tagged PDF, retrieve ITaggedContent from {doc} and call SetTitle({string_literal}) and SetLanguage({string_literal}) before adding any structure elements.

To insert a figure illustration, use ITaggedContent.CreateFigureElement() to obtain an IllustrationElement, configure its properties (AlternativeText, Title, SetTag, SetImage), and append it to ITaggedContent.RootElement via AppendChild.
warnings: Exact fully qualified type name for IllustrationElement may be Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure.FigureElement; the code uses IllustrationElement which could be an alias.
RootElement type is inferred as ILogicalStructureElement; actual concrete type is not explicitly referenced.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a tagged PDF by building a logical structure with header, paragraph, and span elements, setting document metadata, and saving the result.
rules: Obtain the tagged content interface from a Document via {doc}.TaggedContent.
Set document metadata using {doc}.TaggedContent.SetTitle({string_literal}) and SetLanguage({string_literal}).
Create structural elements with the tagged content: HeaderElement via CreateHeaderElement({int}), ParagraphElement via CreateParagraphElement(), SpanElement via CreateSpanElement().
Assign textual content to any structural element using SetText({string_literal}).
Build the logical hierarchy by calling AppendChild(child) on a parent StructureElement (e.g., root or header).
After constructing the structure, persist the PDF with {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: Fully qualified namespace for HeaderElement, ParagraphElement, and SpanElement is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.Tagged; verify if they reside in a sub‑namespace.
ITaggedContent interface location is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.ITaggedContent.

semantic_summary: The example shows how to create a tagged PDF document with various link structure elements, including simple text links, links composed of spans, multiline links, and links that contain an image, and then validates the resulting PDF for PDF/UA compliance.
rules: To build a tagged PDF, obtain ITaggedContent from Document.TaggedContent, set document metadata with SetTitle and SetLanguage, then construct the logical structure by creating elements (ParagraphElement, LinkElement, SpanElement, FigureElement) and appending them to the root StructureElement.
Assign a hyperlink to a LinkElement by setting its Hyperlink property to a new WebHyperlink initialized with the target URL; visible link text can be set via SetText or by adding child SpanElements.
Embed an image inside a link by creating a FigureElement, calling SetImage(path, resolution), setting AlternativeText, and appending the FigureElement to the LinkElement.

Modify layout attributes of a link by retrieving the Layout attribute collection with link.Attributes.GetAttributes(AttributeOwnerStandard.Layout), creating a StructureAttribute with AttributeKey.Placement, setting its value to AttributeName.Placement_Block, and adding it to the collection.
warnings: Fully qualified names for some types (e.g., StructureAttributes, AttributeOwnerStandard, AttributeKey, AttributeName) are inferred from namespaces and may differ in the actual library.
Method signatures such as SetImage(string, int) are assumed based on usage; actual overloads may vary.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a new PDF document, enable tagged PDF features, set document metadata (title and language), and obtain the structure tree root and root structure element for further logical-structure manipulation.
rules: Create a tagged PDF by instantiating {doc}=Aspose.Pdf.Document and retrieving its ITaggedContent via {doc}.TaggedContent.
Set document metadata for a tagged PDF using ITaggedContent.SetTitle({string_literal}) and ITaggedContent.SetLanguage({string_literal}).
Access the logical structure of a tagged PDF through ITaggedContent.StructTreeRootElement and ITaggedContent.RootElement, which return StructTreeRootElement and StructureElement respectively.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a new tagged PDF, set its document title and language, and add header and multilingual paragraph elements using the Aspose.PDF tagging API.
rules: Obtain the tagging interface via {doc}.TaggedContent and set document-level metadata with SetTitle({string_literal}) and SetLanguage({string_literal}).
Create a header element with CreateHeaderElement({int}) on the tagging interface, set its text via SetText({string_literal}), and append it to TaggedContent.RootElement.

Create paragraph elements with CreateParagraphElement(), assign their text via SetText({string_literal}), optionally set their Language property to a locale code ({string_literal}), and append each to TaggedContent.RootElement.
After constructing the tagged structure, persist the PDF using {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: Exact fully qualified names for HeaderElement and ParagraphElement assume they reside in Aspose.Pdf.Tagged.LogicalStructure; verify against the library version used.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a tagged PDF, set document‑level metadata, build a simple logical structure (section and header), and assign accessibility properties to a header element before saving.
rules: To set metadata for a tagged PDF, retrieve {doc}.TaggedContent as {taggedContent} and invoke {taggedContent}.SetTitle({string_literal}) and {taggedContent}.SetLanguage({string_literal}).

Create logical structure elements via {taggedContent}.CreateSectElement() or {taggedContent}.CreateHeaderElement({int}) and attach them to the hierarchy using StructureElement.AppendChild({childElement}).
Assign accessibility properties on a HeaderElement (or other structure elements) by setting Title, Language, AlternativeText, ExpansionText, and ActualText to appropriate string values.
warnings: The example does not add any visual content to pages; structure elements exist without associated page content.
Properties such as Title, Language, etc., are specific to accessibility and may require PDF/UA compliance checks not shown here.

semantic_summary: Shows how to create a tagged PDF containing a styled table, apply cell formatting (background, borders, margins, alignment, text state, spanning), and validate the resulting document for PDF/UA compliance.
rules: Create a tagged PDF: {doc} = new Document(); {doc}.TaggedContent.SetTitle({string_literal}); {doc}.TaggedContent.SetLanguage({string_literal});

Build a table structure under the root element: {table} = {doc}.TaggedContent.CreateTableElement(); {doc}.TaggedContent.RootElement.AppendChild({table}); then create head/body/foot via {table}.CreateTHead(), CreateTBody(), CreateTFoot().
Add rows and cells: for each row create a TableTRElement with CreateTR(); for header cells use CreateTH(), for body/foot cells use CreateTD(); set cell text with SetText({string_literal}).

Style a table cell by setting properties: BackgroundColor = {color}; Border = new BorderInfo(BorderSide.All, {float}, {color}); IsNoBorder = {bool}; Margin = new MarginInfo({float}, {float}, {float}, {float}); Alignment = HorizontalAlignment.{enumValue}; DefaultCellTextState = new TextState { ForegroundColor = {color}, FontSize = {float}, FontStyle = FontStyles.{enumValue}, Font = FontRepository.FindFont({string_literal}) }; IsWordWrapped = {bool}; VerticalAlignment = VerticalAlignment.{enumValue}; ColSpan = {int}; RowSpan = {int};
Validate PDF/UA compliance: {boolResult} = {doc}.Validate({string_literal}, PdfFormat.PDF_UA_1);
warnings: Exact namespace for StructureElement is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.LogicalStructure.StructureElement; verify if a different namespace is used in the library version.
Color class location is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.Color; some versions expose it via Aspose.Pdf.Drawing.Color.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a tagged PDF with a styled table, configure its visual and structural properties, populate header, body and footer rows, save the document, and validate PDF/UA compliance.
rules: To create a styled table in a tagged PDF: obtain {doc}.TaggedContent, call CreateTableElement(), configure properties (BackgroundColor, Border, Alignment, ColumnWidths, DefaultCell*), set layout options (RepeatingRowsCount, RepeatingColumnsCount, RepeatingRowsStyle), then append the TableElement to TaggedContent.RootElement.

To populate a table: use TableTHeadElement.CreateTR() for header rows and TableTHElement.CreateTH() for header cells; use TableTBodyElement.CreateTR() and TableTDElement.CreateTD() for body cells; optionally set AlternativeText on rows and SetText on cells.

To validate PDF/UA compliance after saving: reload the PDF into a new Document instance and call Validate({string_literal_path_to_xml}, PdfFormat.PDF_UA_1) which returns a boolean indicating compliance.
warnings: Enum types such as BorderSide, HorizontalAlignment, TableBroken, ColumnAdjustment, and PdfFormat are inferred from usage; exact namespace may differ.
The placeholder {string_literal_path_to_xml} assumes an existing validation XML file is provided.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a tagged PDF containing a styled table, applying row‑level formatting such as background color, borders, cell defaults, and then validates the document for PDF/UA compliance.
rules: When constructing a tagged PDF table, obtain {doc}.TaggedContent, call CreateTableElement(), and append the resulting TableElement to the root StructureElement.

To style an entire table row uniformly, set properties on the TableTRElement (BackgroundColor, Border, DefaultCellBorder, MinRowHeight, FixedRowHeight, IsInNewPage, IsRowBroken, DefaultCellTextState, DefaultCellPadding, VerticalAlignment) before creating its TableTDElement cells.
After saving a tagged PDF, verify PDF/UA compliance by loading the file into a new Document instance and invoking Validate({string_literal}, PdfFormat.PDF_UA_1).
warnings: The Color type may refer to Aspose.Pdf.Color; ensure the correct namespace is used.
This example requires Aspose.PDF for .NET version 19.6 or later.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a new tagged PDF, set document metadata, add a paragraph with styled text using StructureTextState, and save the result.
rules: To create a tagged PDF, retrieve {doc}.TaggedContent as {taggedContent}, then call {taggedContent}.SetTitle({string_literal}) and {taggedContent}.SetLanguage({string_literal}).

To add styled text, use {taggedContent}.CreateParagraphElement() to obtain a {paragraphElement}, set its {paragraphElement}.StructureTextState.FontSize = {float}, {paragraphElement}.StructureTextState.ForegroundColor = {color}, {paragraphElement}.StructureTextState.FontStyle = {font_style}, then assign text with {paragraphElement}.SetText({string_literal}) and append to {taggedContent}.RootElement.
warnings: StructureTextState properties are applicable only to elements that support text styling; ensure the element type (e.g., ParagraphElement) supports them.
The example relies on default page creation; no explicit page handling is shown.

semantic_summary: The example shows how to modify an existing tagged PDF by setting a document title, adding alternative text and BBox layout attributes to all Figure elements, restructuring the logical hierarchy by moving a Span element into a Paragraph, and finally validating the resulting PDF for PDF/UA compliance.
rules: To set alternative text and a BBox layout attribute for every figure in a tagged PDF: retrieve the root element via {doc}.TaggedContent.RootElement, iterate over rootElement.FindElements<FigureElement>(true), assign FigureElement.AlternativeText = {string_literal}, create a StructureAttribute with AttributeKey.BBox, set its rectangle via SetRectangleValue({rect}), obtain layout attributes with figureElement.Attributes.GetAttributes(AttributeOwnerStandard.Layout) and add the new attribute with SetAttribute.

To relocate a structural element within the tag hierarchy: locate the source element (e.g., SpanElement) and the target parent element (e.g., ParagraphElement) using FindElements<T>(true) on appropriate ancestors, then call sourceElement.ChangeParentElement(targetParentElement).

To validate PDF/UA compliance after modifications: save the document to {output_pdf}, reload it with new Document({output_pdf}), and invoke document.Validate({log_file}, PdfFormat.PDF_UA_1) which returns a boolean indicating compliance.
warnings: The type 'Rectangle' is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.Tagged.Rectangle; if another overload is used the rule may need adjustment.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a new PDF document, access its tagged content, set the document title and language metadata, and save the tagged PDF.
rules: Obtain the tagged content interface via {doc}.TaggedContent and set metadata using {doc}.TaggedContent.SetTitle({string_literal}) and {doc}.TaggedContent.SetLanguage({string_literal}) before saving.
Persist the PDF after configuring tagged metadata with {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: The example creates an empty Document; real scenarios may need to add pages/content before saving.
SetLanguage expects a BCP‑47 language tag (e.g., "en-US").

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a new tagged PDF document, set its title and language, add hierarchical header elements and a paragraph to the logical structure, and save the result.
rules: Obtain the tagged content interface via {doc}.TaggedContent, then set document metadata with SetTitle({string_literal}) and SetLanguage({string_literal}) before building the logical structure.

Create header elements of a specific level using ITaggedContent.CreateHeaderElement({int}) and assign their text with SetText({string_literal}); append each header to the root element using StructureElement.AppendChild.
Create a paragraph element with ITaggedContent.CreateParagraphElement(), set its text via SetText({string_literal}), and append it to the root element.
After constructing the logical structure, persist the tagged PDF by calling {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).
warnings: Namespace for HeaderElement, ParagraphElement, and ITaggedContent is assumed to be Aspose.Pdf.Tagged; verify if they reside in a different namespace in the library version used.

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to create a new PDF, enable tagging, set document metadata (title and language), add a paragraph structure element with text, and save the tagged PDF.
rules: When creating a tagged PDF, retrieve the ITaggedContent from {doc}.TaggedContent, then call SetTitle({string_literal}) and SetLanguage({string_literal}) to define document metadata before adding any structure elements.

To insert textual content, use ITaggedContent.CreateParagraphElement() to obtain a ParagraphElement, set its text with SetText({string_literal}), and attach it to the document hierarchy via ITaggedContent.RootElement.AppendChild({paragraph_element}).
Persist the tagged PDF by invoking {doc}.Save({output_pdf}).

semantic_summary: Demonstrates how to validate a PDF file against the PDF/UA (PDF Universal Accessibility) standard and generate an XML validation report.
rules: Instantiate a {doc} with the path to the input PDF file ({input_pdf}).
Invoke {doc}.Validate({output_log}, Aspose.Pdf.PdfFormat.{enum_value}) to perform validation; the method returns a {bool} indicating whether the document conforms to the specified PDF standard.
warnings: The example relies on a helper method (RunExamples.GetDataDir_AsposePdf_WorkingDocuments) to resolve the data directory, which is not shown in the snippet.
